title: 1015: God
content: The Creator and Redeemer of the world, who reveals himself in Scripture and in Jesus Christ, and who is loved, worshipped and adored by believers. Scripture stresses the personal nature of God, and also his total reliability and trustworthiness. God is the Father of Jesus Christ and of all believers.

title: 1020: God, all-knowing
content:
The omniscience of God is that attribute by which he knows all things past, present and future. What is hidden from human sight is still known by God. Scripture stresses the wisdom of God in all his actions, and often grounds this in his all-embracing knowledge.

God's unique self-knowledge within the Trinity

MAT 11:27 See also JHN 10:15; 1CO 2:10–2:11

The nature of God's knowledge

God's knowledge originates within himself

ISA 40:13–40:14 See also JOB 21:22; ROM 11:33–11:34; 1CO 2:16

God's knowledge is complete

MAT 10:30 parallel LUK 12:7 See also PSA 147:4; ISA 40:26

God knows things that are hidden from human understanding

DEU 29:29 See also JOB 37:15–37:16; DAN 2:22; MAT 24:36 parallel MRK 13:32; ACT 1:7; 2CO 12:2–12:4

God's comprehensive knowledge of people

God's knowledge of people's actions

JOB 34:21 See also JOB 24:23, JOB 31:4; PSA 33:13–33:15, PSA 139:2–139:3; JER 23:24

God's knowledge of people's needs

MAT 6:8 See also MAT 6:31–6:32 parallel LUK 12:29–12:30

God's knowledge of people's hearts and minds

1CH 28:9 Scripture views the heart not primarily as the seat of emotion but more particularly as the seat of the will. See also PSA 44:20–44:21, PSA 139:1–139:2; JER 17:10; EZK 11:5; HEB 4:12–4:13

God's knowledge of individuals

GEN 20:1–20:7 God's knowledge of Abimelech's motives and behaviour towards Abraham's wife Sarah; ; 1SA 16:1–16:12 the LORD to Samuel regarding David's anointing as king; ; ACT 5:1–5:10 God's knowledge of the deception of Ananias and Sapphira

God's knowledge of people's sin

JER 16:17 See also JOB 10:14; PSA 69:5; JER 2:22; HOS 7:2; AMO 5:12

God's foreknowledge

ISA 46:10 See also ISA 42:9, ISA 44:7; DAN 2:28

God's foreknowledge of Jesus Christ's passion

ACT 2:23 See also ACT 3:18, ACT 4:27–4:28

God's foreknowledge of those who would become disciples

ROM 8:29 See also JER 1:5; ROM 11:2; 1PE 1:2

God's foreknowledge of people's free actions

PSA 139:4 See also EXO 3:19; DEU 31:21; 1SA 23:10–23:13

Implications of God's knowledge

God's knowledge ensures that all will be judged fairly

HEB 4:13 See also 1SA 2:3; JOB 34:22–34:23; ECC 12:14; ROM 2:16; 1CO 4:5

God's knowledge ensures that he knows those who are his

2TI 2:19 See also NUM 16:5; EXO 33:12; JOB 23:10JHN 10:14 See also 1CO 8:3; GAL 4:9; 1JN 3:19–3:20; REV 3:8

title: 1025: God, anger of
content:
The punitive and vindicatory reaction, legitimate and controlled, yet awesomely emphatic, of God the righteous judge to unrighteousness in his human creatures. Up to the present, the expression of God's anger and wrath has had the purpose of drawing sinners to repentance and conversion, but this will not be the case at the final judgment.

The nature of God's anger

It is fearsome

NAM 1:6 See also JOS 7:26; EZK 38:18

It is reluctant and short-lived

EXO 34:6 See also ISA 54:7–54:8; NEH 9:16–9:18

It is consistant with his righteous and merciful character

PSA 7:11 See also HAB 3:2

It fulfils God's purposes

JER 23:20 See also JER 30:24

The causes of God's anger

Idolatry and unbelief

NUM 25:3 See also EXO 32:8–32:10; DEU 8:19; JDG 2:10–2:14; 1KI 14:9, 1KI 16:32–16:33, 1KI 22:53; 2KI 23:19 Josiah removes idolatrous shrines; 2CH 28:25, 2CH 34:25; JER 8:19, JER 32:29, JER 44:3; JHN 3:36 the unbelief of rejecting the Son of God; ROM 1:18–1:23, ROM 2:8

Disobedience and disloyalty

JOS 7:1 See also DEU 9:7; 2KI 22:13; 1CH 13:10; PSA 106:29; JER 32:32; ZEC 7:13; EPH 5:6

Ungodly living

God's judgment against the ungodly is a sign of his anger against actions that contradict his righteous character and purposes: 2TI 3:1–3:9; ; JUD 1:14–1:16

Pride, arrogance and hypocrisy

MAT 23:27–23:28 See also 2CH 32:25; PRO 3:34, PRO 8:13; ISA 13:11; HOS 12:14; MAL 4:1

Complaints against, and opposition to, God's purposes

NUM 11:1 See also NUM 14:27, NUM 21:5

Injustice

ZEC 7:9–7:12 See also 2CH 19:7; JER 22:13; EZK 9:9; MAL 3:5; MAT 23:23

The rejection of God's servants

HEB 10:29–10:31 See also DEU 32:35–32:36; PSA 135:14; NEH 9:26; ZEC 7:12; MAT 21:33–21:41; ACT 7:35–7:37

God reveals his anger

In present times

ROM 1:18 See also JER 10:10

On a future ``day of wrath'' which is anticipated

ROM 2:5 See also ISA 13:9, ISA 13:13; EZK 7:19; ZEP 1:15, ZEP 1:18, ZEP 2:2

The consequences of God's anger

God allows those who reject righteousness to remain in their sin

ROM 1:18–1:32 See also 1KI 14:16; 2CH 12:5; NEH 9:28; ISA 54:7–54:8; JER 7:27–7:29, JER 12:7–12:8

Punishment will be experienced by rebellious people

DEU 8:19 See also ISA 59:18; JER 21:14; MAT 18:34–18:35, MAT 25:28–25:30EXO 12:12; NUM 32:13; JOS 7:25–7:26; ISA 13:9–13:13; DAN 5:30; ACT 5:5, ACT 5:101KI 14:15; JER 15:13–15:14, JER 25:7–25:11LAM 2:1–2:9; EZK 38:19–38:21

God will be feared as his holiness and greatness are revealed

ISA 59:18–59:19 See also EZK 38:22–38:23

Ultimately God will purify and restore his people for service

MAL 3:2–3:4 See also JER 15:19–15:21

God is compassionate by nature

2CO 1:3 See also EXO 33:19, EXO 34:6; PSA 86:15, PSA 103:8, PSA 116:5, PSA 119:156; HOS 1:7; JOL 2:13

God shows compassion as a parent

PSA 103:13–103:14 See also ISA 49:15–49:16, ISA 63:15–63:16; JER 31:20

God shows compassion to all

PSA 145:8–145:9

God shows compassion to those in deep trouble

ISA 49:13 See also EXO 22:27; 2KI 13:22–13:23; PSA 40:1–40:2, PSA 40:11–40:12, PSA 69:16–69:17; ISA 30:18; LAM 3:22, LAM 3:32; ZEC 10:6; JAS 5:11

God's compassion is essential to believers' well-being

PSA 119:77 See also PSA 77:8–77:9; ISA 63:15

COL 3:12 See also 2CH 30:9; JER 42:12; ZEC 7:9; LUK 6:36; EPH 4:32

Jesus Christ reflects God's compassion

HEB 4:15 See also LUK 1:72, LUK 15:20

title: 1035: God, faithfulness of
content:
God's perfect loyalty and consistency in being true to his name, his character and his word.

God's faithfulness is an integral part of his nature

NUM 23:19 God's limitless power and steadfast love imply complete faithfulness which contrasts with human nature; LAM 3:22–3:23 See also EXO 34:6; DEU 7:8–7:9, DEU 32:4; ROM 4:21; 2TI 2:13

God is faithful to his name and character

2TI 2:13 See also NEH 9:8; PSA 106:8; 1TH 5:24; HEB 6:13–6:18

God's faithfulness is known through fulfilled promises

JOS 23:14 See also JOS 21:45; 1KI 8:56; 1CH 16:15; PSA 145:13; 2PE 3:9

Examples of God's faithfulness in fulfilled promises

Abraham's fatherhood

GEN 12:2–12:3, GEN 15:4, GEN 21:1–21:2; ; ROM 9:9; ; GAL 4:28; ; HEB 6:13–6:15, HEB 11:11

The building of the temple

2SA 7:12–7:13 parallel 1CH 17:11–17:12; ; 1KI 8:17–8:21 parallel 2CH 6:7–6:11

The exile in Babylon

JER 25:8–25:11 God's faithfulness will not always mean human prosperity. See also 2KI 25:8–25:12 parallels 2CH 36:17–36:21, JER 52:12–52:16; EZR 1:1–1:3; JER 52:27, JER 29:10; DAN 9:2–9:3, DAN 9:15–9:19

God's faithfulness is revealed to the faithful

2SA 22:26 See also GAL 3:14; HEB 10:23

God's faithfulness is constant

ROM 3:3–3:4 God's faithfulness is not negated by human unfaithfulness. See also EZK 12:25; HAB 2:3; 2TI 2:13

Jesus Christ is the ultimate evidence of God's faithfulness

ACT 13:32–13:33 See also JHN 14:9–14:11; ROM 15:8–15:9; 2CO 1:20–1:22; HEB 3:2–3:6; REV 1:5, REV 19:11

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 1040: God, fatherhood of
content:
Primarily signifying God's paternal relationship to Jesus Christ, the term also refers to God's fatherly relationship to his creation, especially to believers as the “children of God”.

Aspects of the fatherhood of God

He is the Creator and provider

1CO 8:6 See also DEU 32:6, DEU 32:18; ISA 64:8; ACT 17:24–17:28

He shows love and compassion

HOS 11:1 See also PSA 103:13; JER 3:19; 2CO 1:3

He exercises his providence and care

PSA 68:5 See also MAT 6:26, MAT 6:31–6:33 parallel LUK 12:29–12:31; MAT 7:11 parallel LUK 11:13

He disciplines and corrects

DEU 8:5 See also PRO 3:11–3:12; HEB 12:5–12:6

God as the Father of Jesus Christ

MAT 3:17 parallels MRK 1:11, LUK 3:22 See also MAT 11:27; LUK 2:49; JHN 5:17–5:18, JHN 17:11, JHN 20:17; ROM 15:6; EPH 1:31PE 1:3 See also 1JN 1:3

God is the father of Israel

He cares for his covenant people

JER 31:9 See also EXO 4:22; DEU 14:1, DEU 32:6, DEU 32:18; ISA 63:16; MAL 2:10

He is the father of the kings of Israel

2SA 7:13–7:14 parallel 1CH 17:12–17:13 This description of God's fatherhood involves the maintenance of a special relationship between God and David's descendants, which ultimately includes Jesus Christ. See also PSA 2:7, PSA 89:26–89:27

God is the father of Christian believers

He has an intimate relationship with believers

MAT 6:9 parallel LUK 11:2 See also JHN 20:17; 2CO 6:18; GAL 3:26; 1JN 2:13

He adopts believers into his family

JHN 1:12–1:13 See also ROM 8:14–8:17; GAL 4:5–4:7; 1JN 3:1

The fatherhood of God has implications for the church

Character: children are to reproduce the Father's likeness

MAT 5:48

Unity: one Father means one family

EPH 4:3–4:6 See also MAL 2:10; MAT 23:9; EPH 3:14–3:15

Status: believers are related to Jesus Christ

MAT 12:50 parallels MRK 3:35, LUK 8:21 See also ROM 8:29; HEB 2:11

Inheritance: believers are heirs of the Father's kingdom

ROM 8:17 See also LUK 12:32; GAL 3:29; COL 1:12; TIT 3:7

Submission: believers are to revere the Father

1PE 1:17 See also MAT 6:9 parallel LUK 11:2; EPH 3:14

title: 1045: God, glory of
content:
The revelation of God's power and characteristics, sometimes accompanied by visible phenomena.

Glory as an attribute of God

PSA 24:7–24:8 See also PSA 29:3; JHN 11:40; ACT 7:2; REV 19:1

Visible phenomena accompanying God's glory

The appearance of God's glory

HAB 3:4 See also DEU 5:24; 2SA 22:8–22:16 parallel PSA 18:7–18:15; PSA 104:1; EZK 1:26–1:28

God's glory as a cloud

EXO 24:15–24:16 See also EXO 33:9–33:10; ISA 4:5; MAT 17:5 parallels MRK 9:7, LUK 9:34; LUK 21:27 parallels MAT 24:30, MRK 13:26

God's glory in the tabernacle and the temple

2CH 5:13–5:14 parallel 1KI 8:10–8:11 The Jewish term for the outward sign of God's presence in the temple is “Shekinah”. See also EXO 40:34–40:35; 2CH 7:1–7:3; EZK 8:4, EZK 9:3, EZK 10:19, EZK 43:1–43:5; REV 15:8

God's glory revealed

In Jesus Christ

HEB 1:3 See also ISA 49:3; JHN 1:14, JHN 13:31–13:32, JHN 17:5; 2CO 4:6; 2PE 1:17

In his people

COL 1:27 See also ISA 60:19–60:21, ISA 62:3; 2CO 3:18; EPH 3:21

In the whole world

ISA 6:3 See also NUM 14:21; PSA 57:5, PSA 57:11 parallel PSA 108:5; HAB 2:14, HAB 3:3

In heaven

REV 21:23 See also ROM 8:17; HEB 2:9; 1PE 5:10

In his name

NEH 9:5 See also DEU 28:58; 1CH 29:13; PSA 8:1, PSA 79:9

In his works

PSA 111:3 See also PSA 19:1; ISA 12:5, ISA 35:2; JHN 11:40–11:44, JHN 17:4

In his kingdom

PSA 145:11–145:12 See also 1CH 29:11; MAT 6:13; 1TH 2:12

God's glory cannot be fully seen

1TI 6:15–6:16 EXO 33:18–33:20; ; JDG 6:22–6:23, JDG 13:20–13:22; ; ISA 6:5; ; JHN 1:18

God's glory experienced

ACT 7:55 See also EXO 3:2 Moses; ISA 6:1–6:4 Isaiah; LUK 2:9 the shepherds

The effects of God's glory

God's glory is acknowledged

PSA 72:19 See also PSA 29:2; ROM 11:36; REV 4:11, REV 5:13, REV 19:1

God's glory brings guilt and fear

ROM 3:23 See also ISA 2:10, ISA 2:19, ISA 2:21; REV 11:13, REV 15:8

title: 1050: God, goodness of
content:
All God's perfect qualities are made freely available to all for the benefit of the whole world.

God alone is good

MRK 10:18 parallels MAT 19:17, LUK 18:19

God demonstrates his goodness

In his actions

PSA 119:68 See also 2CH 30:18; NEH 9:20; PSA 73:1, PSA 143:10; ACT 10:38; ROM 2:4

In his work of creation

1TI 4:4 See also GEN 1:4, GEN 1:31; PSA 145:9

In his love

PSA 86:5 See also 1CH 16:34 parallel PSA 106:1; PSA 25:7–25:8, PSA 69:16, PSA 100:5

In his gifts

JAS 1:17 See also NUM 13:27; DEU 8:7, DEU 26:11; HEB 9:11

In his promises

JOS 23:14–23:15 See also 2SA 7:28 parallel 1CH 17:26; 1KI 8:56; JER 29:10

In his commands

PSA 119:39 See also PSA 19:7; ROM 7:12, ROM 7:16, ROM 12:2

God's goodness is to be experienced

God is good to those who trust him

PSA 34:8; NAM 1:7 See also EXO 33:19; 2CH 7:10; JOB 42:11; PSA 31:19–31:20, PSA 84:11, PSA 86:17; LAM 3:25; AMO 5:14–5:15; 1PE 2:3

God's people can rely on his goodness

JER 32:40 See also PSA 23:6, PSA 27:13; MAT 7:11 parallel LUK 11:13; PHP 1:6

God works positively for good in unfavourable circumstances

GEN 50:20 See also ACT 2:23–2:24; ROM 8:28; 2CO 4:17; HEB 12:10

God's goodness is to be praised

PSA 135:3 See also DEU 8:10; 2CH 5:13, 2CH 7:3; PSA 86:5, PSA 136:1, PSA 145:7

title: 1055: God, grace and mercy of
content:
The qualities of God's character by which he shows himself compassionate, accepting, and generous to sinful human beings, shielding them from his wrath, forgiving them, and bestowing on them his righteousness so that they can live and grow in faith and obedience. Grace and mercy are particularly expressed through God's covenant with his chosen people and through Jesus Christ's atoning death on the cross.

God's grace and mercy are made known in Jesus Christ

JHN 1:16–1:17

The abundance of God's grace and mercy

EPH 2:4–2:8 See also 2SA 24:14 parallel 1CH 21:13; PSA 69:13, PSA 84:11, PSA 102:13; ROM 2:4, ROM 5:17, ROM 9:23; 1TI 1:14

God's grace and mercy are always unearned and unmerited

DEU 7:7–7:8 See also DEU 9:5–9:6; EZK 36:22; DAN 9:18; ROM 9:16; EPH 1:6, EPH 2:8–2:9, EPH 3:8; TIT 3:5

God's grace and mercy are a source of blessing

GEN 21:1–21:2 See also GEN 33:11; 1SA 2:21

God's grace and mercy are expressed in the covenant relationship

JER 31:3 Hebrew “hesed” (translated as “love”, “loving-kindness”, “unfailing love”) expresses specifically the grace and mercy that underlie the covenant relationship. See also EXO 34:6; DEU 7:9, DEU 7:12; NEH 9:17; PSA 6:4; ISA 55:3; LAM 3:22, LAM 3:32

Salvation comes by grace

TIT 2:11 See also MRK 10:25–10:27 Jesus Christ's words to his disciples; ROM 5:15; EPH 1:5–1:6, EPH 2:8; 2TI 1:9–1:10

This is ultimately shown in the cross of Jesus Christ

ROM 3:24–3:25 See also ROM 5:8; EPH 1:7; HEB 2:9; 2PE 1:10–1:11

God's grace and mercy are offered to sinners

Punishment is withheld

EZR 9:13 See also 2KI 13:22–13:23; EZK 20:15–20:17; HOS 11:8; JOL 2:13

Sin is forgiven

MIC 7:18; 1JN 1:9 See also PSA 32:5; PRO 28:13; ISA 55:7; JER 3:12, JER 33:8; DAN 9:9

Sinners' prayers are heard

PSA 51:1; LUK 18:13–18:14 See also PSA 6:2–6:3, PSA 123:3; HAB 3:2

The favour of God

Examples of those who found favour with God

GEN 4:4 Abel; ; GEN 6:8 Noah; ; EXO 33:12 Moses; ; JDG 6:17–6:18 Gideon; ; 1SA 2:26 Samuel; ; EZR 7:27–7:28 Ezra; ; LUK 1:30 Mary; ; LUK 2:52 Jesus Christ; ; ACT 7:46 David

Examples of those who seek the favour of God

EXO 32:11 Moses; ; 2KI 13:4 Jehoahaz; ; 2CH 33:12 Manasseh; NEH 5:19, NEH 13:31; DAN 9:17 Daniel

Promises relating to the favour of God

LEV 26:9 It is given to the obedient (see Lev 26:3); ; PSA 5:12 It is given to the righteous; ; PSA 30:5 It lasts for a lifetime.

The OT predicts a future age of God's favour

ISA 49:8 See also ISA 60:10, ISA 61:2; EZK 36:9

Implications for believers of the grace and mercy of God

God's grace and mercy are to influence the character and conduct of believers

ROM 6:1–6:2 See also ACT 11:23; ROM 6:14, ROM 12:1; TIT 2:11–2:12

Believers must humbly rely on God's grace and mercy

GAL 2:21 See also PRO 3:34; JAS 4:6; JON 2:8; ROM 2:4; 2CO 6:1–6:2

God's grace strengthens believers for service

EPH 3:7–3:8 See also 1CO 3:10; 2CO 12:7–12:9; GAL 1:15–1:16; EPH 4:7, EPH 4:11

Illustrations of God's grace and mercy

God shows grace to Noah, saving him from the flood and afterwards establishing a covenant with him: GEN 6:8, GEN 6:22, GEN 9:9–9:11; ; JON 1:1–1:3, JON 1:17, JON 2:1–2:3, JON 3:1–3:10 God shows mercy in saving first Jonah and then (following their repentance) the people of Nineveh; ; LUK 15:11–15:32 The forgiving love of the prodigal's father illustrates the divine mercy of God.

title: 1060: God, greatness of
content:
God's nature, deeds and attributes are incomparable, for which he deserves all praise.

God is great

JER 10:6 See also 1CH 16:25 parallel PSA 96:4; 1CH 29:11; NEH 1:5; ISA 12:6

God's name is great

EZK 36:23 See also JOS 7:9; 1SA 12:22; 1KI 8:42 parallel 2CH 6:32

God is a great king

PSA 47:2 See also PSA 47:7, PSA 48:2; MAL 1:14; MAT 5:35; 1TI 6:15

God's deeds are great

He does great things

PSA 136:4 See also JDG 2:7; ISA 55:9; JER 32:19; LUK 1:49; EPH 3:20

His acts are powerful

DEU 9:29 God's powerful acts are often associated with the exodus. See also EXO 15:7; DEU 9:261SA 19:5; 2SA 23:10ISA 40:26; JER 32:17 NAM 1:3; EPH 1:19–1:20 His great power at work in the resurrection

God's attributes are great

His great love

PSA 108:4 See also NUM 14:19; 2CH 1:8; LAM 3:22; EPH 2:4; 1JN 3:1

His great mercy

1CH 21:13 See also NEH 9:31; ROM 5:17; 1PE 1:3

His great anger

2KI 22:13 See also PSA 90:11, PSA 102:10; JER 21:5, JER 32:37

God's gifts are great

He bestows great gifts

DAN 5:18 See also GEN 12:2; 1SA 26:25; 1CH 29:25

He gives great joy

NEH 12:43 See also EZR 6:22; PSA 4:7, PSA 126:3

The effects of God's greatness

God's power is known in the nations

PSA 126:2 See also 2CH 9:6, 2CH 20:6; PSA 99:2; DAN 4:3

People are astonished

LUK 9:43 See also HAB 1:5; LUK 5:26

God must be praised for his greatness

PSA 48:1 See also DEU 32:3; PSA 89:1, PSA 96:4, PSA 145:3

God's greatness makes him unique

There is no-one like him

PSA 150:2 See also 2SA 7:22; PSA 71:19, PSA 86:10, PSA 145:3

He is greater than other gods

EXO 18:11 See also DEU 4:7, DEU 10:17; 2CH 2:5; PSA 135:5; 1CO 8:4–8:6

God is greater than human power and pride

JOB 33:12 See also 2CH 32:7–32:8; ISA 40:6–40:7; 1JN 3:20

title: 1065: God, holiness of
content:
The moral excellence of God that unifies his attributes and is expressed through his actions, setting him apart from all others. Believers are called to be holy as God is holy.

God's nature is holy

He is perfect

DEU 32:4; ISA 6:3; REV 4:8 See also 2SA 22:31; JOB 6:10; PSA 18:30, PSA 22:3, PSA 71:22, PSA 78:41; ISA 41:14, ISA 43:15; HAB 1:13; JHN 17:11; REV 6:10

He is uniquely holy

1SA 2:2 See also EXO 15:11; PSA 77:13; ISA 40:25; REV 15:4

God's name is holy

EZK 36:21–36:23 See also LEV 22:32; 1CH 16:35, 1CH 29:16; PSA 33:21, PSA 97:12; ISA 57:15; EZK 39:25; LUK 1:49

God's dwelling-place is holy

ISA 57:15 David's palace was regarded as holy because of the presence of the ark See also 2CH 8:11, 2CH 30:27; PSA 2:6, PSA 3:4, PSA 5:7, PSA 11:4, PSA 15:1, PSA 20:6, PSA 47:8, PSA 48:1, PSA 65:4; ISA 63:15; JOL 3:17; OBA 1:16–1:17; JON 2:4; MIC 1:2; HAB 2:20; ZEC 2:13; ACT 21:28 The Jews accused Paul of defiling the temple area by bringing in Gentiles; EPH 2:21–2:22 the church as the dwelling in which God lives by his Spirit; HEB 10:19–10:22; REV 22:19

God's holiness is revealed in his righteous activity

ISA 5:16 See also JDG 5:11; 1SA 12:7; PSA 77:13, PSA 145:17; DAN 9:14, DAN 9:16; ZEP 3:5

God's holiness affects worship

It is celebrated in worship

PSA 99:5 See also 1CH 16:29; PSA 29:2, PSA 99:5, PSA 103:1, PSA 105:3, PSA 145:21; ISA 6:3

Coming before a holy God requires preparation

EXO 3:5 See also EXO 29:37; PSA 24:3–24:4; 1CO 11:28; HEB 10:1–10:2, HEB 10:22

Special requirements and tasks are given to worship leaders

LEV 21:7–21:8 the priests; ; LEV 21:10–21:15 the high priest; EXO 28:1–28:43; ; LEV 21:16–21:23; 2CH 29:5 the Levites

God's holiness is to be seen in his people

God's people are to be holy because he is holy

title: 1070: God, joy of
content:
God rejoices in the well-being and faithfulness of his covenant people, and in the repentance and conversion of sinners. He brings joy to his people, who rejoice in his presence and faithfulness.

His chosen servant

Jesus Christ

title: 1075: God, justice of
content:
The moral righteousness of God is revealed in his laws and expressed in his judicial acts. God's commands and judgments meet perfect standards of justice, and his apportioning of punishments and rewards is also perfectly just. God's justice is impartial. Special praise is his for vindicating the penitent and the needy who have no human champions. Ultimately, all God's ways will be seen as just and equitable.

God's justice displays his righteousness

It conforms to his moral law

JOB 34:12 See also DEU 32:4; PSA 9:16, PSA 11:7; ZEP 3:5; ROM 2:2; 2TI 2:13; REV 15:3

It is seen in his perfect will

PSA 99:4 See also PSA 40:8; ISA 53:10; MAT 6:10, MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42; HEB 10:9–10:10

PSA 33:5–33:15 See also NEH 9:33

2CH 36:15 See also 2KI 17:23; NEH 9:29–9:30; JER 7:13; JON 3:4; HEB 12:25

content:
God is alive and active. In contrast to idols, he is uncreated and the source of all continuing life.

content:
The deepest possible expression of God's character. Though God loves all people, he is especially committed to sacrificial, loyal relationships with his people.

EXO 34:6–34:7 See also NEH 9:17; PSA 103:8; JOL 2:13; JON 4:2; 1JN 3:1

title: 1090: God, majesty of
content:

title: 1095: God, patience of
content:
God shows forbearance in his character, decisions and actions. Above all, he waits patiently for people to turn to him for salvation.

GEN 45:5–45:9 in the life of Joseph; ISA 41:2, ISA 41:25, ISA 45:1, ISA 45:13, ISA 46:11, ISA 48:14; ACT 18:21, ACT 21:14; ; ROM 1:10, ROM 15:32; ; 1CO 4:19

title: 1120: God, repentance of
content:
A change in God's plan or intention, often in response to human repentance, but without implying any fault or moral imperfection on God's part.

God's repentance may show his pain and sorrow

GEN 6:6–6:7 God's change of purpose here involves his intention to punish. See also 1SA 15:11, 1SA 15:35

God's repentance may show his compassion

JON 4:2 God's change of purpose here involves his compassion for sinners: “relent” in this and other passages may also be translated “repent”. See also DEU 32:36; 2SA 24:15–24:16 parallel 1CH 21:14–21:15; PSA 106:45; ISA 38:1–38:5; HOS 11:8; AMO 7:1–7:6

God may repent in response to human repentance

JER 18:8 See also EXO 32:14; 2CH 12:1–12:8; JER 26:13, JER 26:19; JOL 2:13; JON 3:6–3:10

God never repents of his oath or changes his character

NUM 23:19; ; PSA 110:4; ; HEB 6:17–6:18

title: 1125: God, righteousness of
content:
An aspect of God's nature which expresses his unique moral perfection and his readiness to save sinners. It is made known especially through the gospel of Jesus Christ.

God's nature is righteous

PSA 119:137 See also DEU 32:4; PSA 48:10, PSA 97:2, PSA 119:142, PSA 145:17; ISA 45:21; JER 12:1; JHN 17:25

God's righteousness shows his sovereignty

PSA 71:19 See also JOB 37:23; PSA 36:6, PSA 97:6; ISA 5:16

God's righteousness is eternal

PSA 111:3 See also PSA 112:3, PSA 112:9, PSA 119:142, PSA 119:160; ISA 51:8

God's actions are righteous

DAN 9:14 See also JDG 5:11; 1SA 12:7; JER 9:24; REV 15:3

God's rule is righteous

PSA 9:8 See also 2SA 23:3–23:4; PSA 96:13, PSA 99:4; JER 9:23–9:24

God's righteous acts are saving acts

PSA 65:5 See also PSA 40:10, PSA 116:4–116:6, PSA 129:4; ISA 41:10, ISA 46:13; DAN 9:16; MIC 6:5

God's righteousness vindicates his people

PSA 35:24 See also PSA 4:2–4:3, PSA 7:9, PSA 9:4, PSA 103:6; ISA 50:8; MIC 7:9; ROM 8:33

God's righteousness shows his faithfulness

NEH 9:8 See also PSA 4:1; ZEP 3:5; ZEC 8:8

God's righteousness shows his justice

JER 11:20 See also GEN 18:25; JOB 8:3; PSA 11:7, PSA 50:6, PSA 51:4; PRO 21:12; ECC 3:17; 2TI 4:8

God's righteousness is seen in his judgments

PSA 7:11 See also 2CH 12:5–12:6; EZR 9:15; ISA 10:22, ISA 28:17; LAM 1:18; MAL 3:5; ROM 2:2, ROM 2:5

God's laws show his righteousness

PSA 19:8–19:9 See also DEU 4:8; PSA 33:4, PSA 119:7, PSA 119:144; ROM 1:32, ROM 7:12, ROM 8:4

God's righteousness contrasts with human unrighteousness

ROM 3:5 See also EXO 9:27; NEH 9:33; JOB 4:17, JOB 9:2; DAN 9:7; ROM 10:3

God's righteousness is revealed primarily in Jesus Christ

ROM 3:21–3:22 See also ISA 11:2–11:5; JER 23:6; ZEC 9:9; ACT 3:14; ROM 10:4; 1CO 1:30; 2PE 1:1; 1JN 2:1

God's righteousness is revealed in the gospel

ROM 1:17 See also PHP 3:9

God gives his righteousness to believers

ROM 4:22–4:24 See also GEN 15:6; JOB 33:26; HOS 10:12; ROM 3:22, ROM 4:3–4:8, ROM 5:17, ROM 10:4; 2CO 5:21

The Holy Spirit reveals God's righteousness

GAL 5:5 See also JHN 16:8, JHN 16:10; ROM 14:17

God's righteousness is to be sought after

MAT 6:33 See also ISA 51:1; ZEP 2:3

God's righteousness is a pattern for human living

1JN 3:7 See also GEN 18:19; HOS 14:9; EPH 4:24; 1JN 2:29, 1JN 3:12

God's righteousness is worthy of praise

ISA 24:15–24:16 See also PSA 7:17, PSA 22:31, PSA 35:28, PSA 51:14, PSA 71:15, PSA 71:24, PSA 145:7

title: 1130: God, sovereignty of
content:
The fact that God is free and able to do all that he wills; that he reigns over all creation and that his will is the final cause of all things. This is often expressed in the language of kingship.

God is free to do all he wills

PSA 135:6 See also PSA 115:3; ISA 46:10; DAN 4:35; ROM 9:19–9:21

God is able to do whatever he wills

LUK 1:37 Gabriel speaking to Mary. See also JOB 42:2; MAT 19:26; EPH 3:20

God cannot be successfully opposed

JOB 42:2 From Job's prayer of submission and repentance. See also 1SA 2:10; 2CH 20:6; JOB 9:12; ECC 7:13; ISA 43:13, ISA 45:9–45:10; ACT 5:39

God rules and reigns

He is King

PSA 29:10 See also 1CH 16:31; PSA 47:2; ISA 6:5, ISA 43:15; JER 10:7, JER 10:10; ZEC 14:9; 1TI 1:17, 1TI 6:15; REV 15:3, REV 19:6

He is Lord of heaven and earth

DEU 4:39 See also GEN 24:3; DEU 10:14; JOS 2:11; 1CH 29:11; NEH 9:6; PSA 121:2, PSA 134:3; LUK 10:21; ACT 17:24

His throne is a symbol of his sovereignty

EXO 17:16; ; PSA 45:6, PSA 93:2, PSA 123:1; ; ISA 6:1 Isaiah's commission; ; ISA 66:1; ; JER 49:38; ; LAM 5:19; ; EZK 1:26; ; DAN 7:9 Daniel's dream; ; MAT 5:34, MAT 19:28 Jesus Christ teaches his disciples about things to come; ; ACT 7:49; ; HEB 1:8, HEB 8:1, HEB 12:2; ; REV 4:2, REV 20:11

God's sovereignty extends over all things

He is sovereign over creation

REV 4:11 See also PSA 93:1; ISA 40:22, ISA 41:18–41:19

He is sovereign over human life

1CH 29:12 Part of David's prayer on the occasion of gifts being brought to the temple. See also 2CH 25:8; LUK 1:51–1:53; ACT 18:21; JAS 4:15

He is sovereign over the minutest details of life

MAT 10:29–10:30 parallel LUK 12:6–12:7

He is sovereign in electing his people

EPH 1:11 See also ROM 8:29, ROM 9:11, ROM 9:18

He is sovereign in the life and salvation of his people

1CO 1:30 See also JER 18:6; 1CO 12:11; PHP 2:13; JAS 1:18

He is sovereign over the sufferings of believers

PHP 1:29; ; 1PE 3:17

He is sovereign over world history

PRO 21:1 See also EXO 9:16; PSA 22:28; JER 18:7–18:10; DAN 4:35

God is sovereign over all other gods and over demonic forces

God is supreme over all gods

PSA 95:3 “gods” are part of the created order and subject to God's sovereignty. See also EXO 18:11; DEU 10:17; 1CH 16:26; PSA 96:5; DAN 2:47; COL 1:16

Satan is defeated through God's sovereign purposes at work in Jesus Christ

COL 2:15 See also JHN 12:31; ROM 16:20; 2TH 2:8; HEB 2:14; 2PE 2:4; 1JN 3:8; JUD 1:6; REV 12:7–12:10

title: 1135: God, suffering of
content:
God suffers on account of the failures and pain of his people, and especially through his Son, Jesus Christ. Scripture often likens God to a parent who suffers pain and grief on account of children.

God feels real suffering

He suffers pain because people sin

GEN 6:6 See also 1SA 15:10–15:11, 1SA 15:35

He is angered because people sin

PSA 78:58 See also DEU 32:15–32:16; JDG 2:12–2:13; 1KI 21:22; EZK 8:17; HOS 12:14

God identifies with suffering

He suffers with his chosen people

ISA 63:9 See also EXO 3:7; JDG 10:16; 2CH 36:15

He suffers with all humanity

ISA 16:11 parallel JER 48:31 See also 2SA 24:15–24:17 parallel 1CH 21:14–21:17 God suffers even with those who endure punishment from his own hand; ISA 16:9; JER 48:32–48:36; HOS 11:8–11:9

He suffers as a parent

JER 31:20 An indication that God suffers in both disciplining and loving. See also ISA 42:14, ISA 49:15; HOS 11:1–11:4

God's plan of salvation causes him suffering

He suffered in giving his Son

ROM 8:32 See also ISA 7:13–7:14; JHN 3:16; ROM 3:25–3:26, ROM 8:3; GAL 4:4; HEB 1:2; 1JN 4:14

He suffers because he endures the rejection his Son suffers

LUK 10:16 See also 1PE 2:4

God's creation reflects his suffering

ROM 8:22 See also ROM 8:19–8:21

God's people share his suffering

ROM 8:17 There is a sense in which the sufferings of Father and Son are indistinguishable. See also MAT 16:24 parallels MRK 8:34, LUK 9:23; MAT 20:22–20:23 parallel MRK 10:38–10:39; PHP 1:29; 1PE 2:21

title: 1140: God, the eternal
content:
God transcends all limitations of time and endures for ever.

God endures for ever

NEH 9:5 See also DEU 32:40, DEU 33:27; JOB 36:26; PSA 48:14, PSA 102:12; ISA 40:28; HAB 1:12

God transcends all concepts of time

REV 1:8 See also PSA 90:4; ISA 41:4; 1CO 2:7; HEB 13:8; 2PE 3:8; REV 11:17, REV 22:13

He existed before time

PSA 90:2 See also PRO 8:23; JHN 17:5, JHN 17:24; EPH 1:4; 1PE 1:20

He will exist after time

HEB 1:11–1:12 See also PSA 102:26–102:27

The eternity of God's qualities

His love

1CH 16:34 parallel PSA 106:1 See also 2CH 5:13; PSA 103:17; JER 31:3; HOS 2:19

His faithfulness

PSA 100:5 See also PSA 117:2, PSA 146:6

His power

PSA 66:7 See also ROM 1:20; 1PE 5:11

His righteousness

PSA 111:3 See also PSA 112:3, PSA 119:142; DAN 9:24

God's word is eternal

PSA 119:89 See also MRK 13:31; 1PE 1:24–1:25; ISA 40:8

God's covenant is eternal

GEN 17:7 See also NUM 18:19; PSA 105:10; ISA 24:5, ISA 54:10, ISA 55:3; JER 32:40

God's kingdom is eternal

PSA 145:13 See also EXO 15:18; PSA 9:7, PSA 45:6; LAM 5:19; DAN 4:3, DAN 7:14, DAN 7:27; 2PE 1:11

God's gifts are eternal

ROM 11:29

He gives eternal life

JHN 10:28 See also JHN 5:24, JHN 6:47; ROM 2:7, ROM 6:23

He gives eternal salvation

ISA 45:17 See also HEB 5:9, HEB 9:12

He gives eternal joy

ISA 35:10 See also PSA 16:11; ISA 51:11, ISA 61:7

He gives eternal possessions

GEN 48:4 See also GEN 17:8

title: 1145: God, transcendence of
content:
God is far above, beyond and outside the created order.

God is outside and above all creation

PSA 113:4; ISA 40:22 1KI 8:27; ; 2CH 2:6; JOB 37:23 Elihu declares the LORD to be beyond reach; ; PSA 8:1, PSA 57:5, PSA 57:11, PSA 97:9, PSA 108:5, PSA 148:13; ; ISA 33:5; ; ACT 7:49, ACT 17:24; ; EPH 1:20–1:21 Jesus Christ is above all, in the place of highest honour and authority; ; EPH 4:6; ; HEB 7:26

God is able also to draw near to his creatures

ISA 57:15

The invisibility of God

God is invisible to people and hidden from their sight

EXO 33:20; ISA 45:15 See also EXO 3:6JOB 9:11, JOB 23:8–23:9 PSA 10:1, PSA 13:1, PSA 89:46; JHN 1:18 Jesus Christ has made God known; JHN 5:37; COL 1:15 Jesus Christ is the image of God; 1TI 1:17, 1TI 6:16; 1JN 4:12

OT saints who saw God

EXO 33:11 These exceptions indicate that God's invisibility is not inherent, but the result of sinful people's inability to look upon a holy God. See also GEN 32:30; EXO 24:10; JDG 13:22; ISA 6:5

God is made visible in Jesus Christ

JHN 1:18, JHN 14:9; ; 2CO 4:4, 2CO 4:6; ; COL 1:15

Redeemed people will be able to look at God's face

MAT 5:8; ; 1CO 13:12; ; 1JN 3:2; ; REV 22:4

God's ways are beyond human understanding

ISA 55:8–55:9 See also DEU 29:29; JOB 5:9, JOB 11:7, JOB 36:22–36:23; PSA 139:1–139:6; PRO 25:2; ECC 3:11; ISA 40:28; ROM 11:33–11:34; 1CO 2:11, 1CO 2:16

title: 1150: God, truth of
content:
God's unique integrity is displayed in his perfection of character and attributes. It is further emphasised by the consistent truth of his words and works, supremely shown in Jesus Christ.

God is the one and only true God

He alone is God

JER 10:10 See also DEU 4:39; 2CH 15:3; ISA 43:10–43:11, ISA 45:5–45:6, ISA 45:14, ISA 45:21; ZEC 14:9; JHN 1:18, JHN 17:3; REV 6:10

The contrast with other ``gods''

ROM 1:25 See also EXO 15:11; DEU 4:35, DEU 32:39; PSA 86:8; ISA 43:3; 1TH 1:9

The contrast with earthly rulers

JER 10:6–10:8

God is characterised by truth

PSA 31:5 See also PSA 40:10, PSA 43:3; ISA 65:16; JHN 3:33, JHN 7:28, JHN 8:26; 1JN 5:20; REV 15:3

God's truth is demonstrated in the revelation of himself in Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ is ``the truth''

JHN 14:6 In the light of this statement it may be concluded that truth is actually defined by the person and work of Jesus Christ. See also 1JN 5:20

Jesus Christ and the Spirit bring truth

JHN 1:14, JHN 14:16–14:17; ; ROM 15:8

God's words are truthful

His words are consistently true and reliable

NUM 23:19 See also 1SA 15:29; PSA 12:6, PSA 33:4, PSA 119:151, PSA 119:160; ROM 3:4; TIT 1:2; HEB 6:18; JAS 1:17

His words of promise are totally true and trustworthy

PSA 132:11 See also 2TI 2:13 The guarantee of God's faithfulness is the truthfulness of his promises which depend in turn upon the truth that characterises God himself; HEB 7:21; PSA 110:4

God's people live in the light of his truth

PSA 86:11 See also PSA 26:3, PSA 119:141–119:142; DAN 9:13; JHN 17:17

title: 1155: God, truthfulness of
content:
To call the LORD “the true God” means that he alone has in his being the fulness of deity and is worthy to be worshipped as God. He and his word are a trustworthy foundation for life because he speaks the truth and is utterly reliable and consistent in his character, his revelation of himself, his promises and his pronouncements.

Titles reflecting God's truthfulness

The true God

2CH 15:3; ; JER 10:10; ; 1TH 1:9

The God of truth

PSA 31:5; ; ISA 65:16

Jesus Christ is ``the truth''

JHN 14:6 See also JHN 1:14, JHN 1:17, JHN 6:32 Jesus Christ calls himself “the true bread” meaning that he is real, substantial, reliable and life-giving; 1JN 5:20; REV 3:7, REV 3:14

The Spirit of truth

JHN 14:17, JHN 15:26, JHN 16:13; ; 1JN 4:6, 1JN 5:6

God is true to his character and his word

He speaks the truth

ISA 45:19 See also 2SA 7:28; PSA 33:4, PSA 119:160; JHN 17:17; REV 21:5, REV 22:6

He does not lie

NUM 23:19 See also ROM 3:4; 2TI 2:13; TIT 1:2; HEB 6:18

He is true to himself and his promises

DEU 32:4; ; PSA 25:10, PSA 33:4, PSA 145:13, PSA 146:6; ; LAM 3:23; ; 2TI 2:13

The truthfulness of God undergirds the law

PSA 119:142 See also PSA 19:7, PSA 119:151, PSA 119:160; MAL 2:6–2:7

The truthfulness of God undergirds the covenant

PSA 25:10 The Hebrew word translated “faithful” can also be translated “true”. The word is particularly associated with God steadfastly upholding his covenant promises. See also PSA 145:13; HOS 2:19–2:20; 2TI 2:13

The truthfulness of God undergirds the prophetic word

EZK 12:25 See also ISA 55:11; JER 1:12, JER 28:9; EZK 33:33; DAN 2:45

The truthfulness of God undergirds his promises

JOS 23:14–23:15 See also NUM 23:19; 1KI 8:20; PSA 105:42; ROM 4:20–4:21

The truthfulness of God undergirds his judgment

PSA 96:13 See also PSA 98:9; ISA 11:3–11:5; ROM 2:2

The truthfulness of God undergirds the teaching of Jesus Christ

JHN 1:17 See also MAT 5:18, MAT 22:16 parallels MRK 12:14, LUK 20:21; MRK 3:28; LUK 4:24; JHN 1:51, JHN 8:31–8:32, JHN 18:37

title: 1160: God, unchangeableness of
content:
God's nature, plans and actions do not change even though he is active and his relationships do not remain static. His moral consistency guarantees his commitment to unchanging principles.

God's being is unchangeable

PSA 102:27 See also HEB 1:12; MAL 3:6; HEB 13:8; JAS 1:17

God's characteristics do not change

God's love is constant

PSA 89:2 See also PSA 136:1–136:26; 2TI 2:13

God's purposes and plans do not change

HEB 6:17 See also NUM 23:19; 1SA 15:29; PRO 19:21; JER 44:29; HEB 7:21; PSA 110:4

God does not revoke his promises

PSA 132:11 See also PSA 145:13; HEB 4:1

God's word does not change

ISA 40:8 See also PSA 119:89; MAT 5:18; 1PE 1:23–1:25

God's failure to inflict promised judgment is a sign of his unchanging grace

HOS 11:8 See also EXO 32:10–32:14; JON 3:10, JON 4:2

title: 1165: God, uniqueness of
content:
No-one and nothing is comparable to the triune God in his nature or comparable to him in his character and activity.

There is no God except the LORD

ISA 45:5–45:6 God alone stands outside nature as its Creator. All other beings are created by him. See also DEU 4:35, DEU 6:4, DEU 32:3; 1KI 8:60; PSA 83:18, PSA 86:10; ISA 43:10–43:11, ISA 44:6–44:8, ISA 45:18; 1CO 8:4–8:6; EPH 4:6; 1TI 2:5

There is no-one like God

PSA 89:6–89:8 See also EXO 8:10; ISA 40:18

In his creative power

ISA 40:25–40:26 See also JHN 1:3; NEH 9:6; ACT 14:15; COL 1:16; REV 4:11

In his mighty acts

PSA 86:10 See also PSA 135:5–135:6

In his character and glory

EXO 15:11 See also 2SA 7:22; 1CH 29:11

In his ability to save

DEU 33:26 See also ISA 45:20–45:22; JER 10:5–10:6

In his covenant love

1KI 8:23 See also 2SA 7:22–7:23 parallel 1CH 17:20–17:21

In his sovereignty

ZEC 14:9 See also DEU 4:39; 1TI 1:17

Implications of God's uniqueness

God alone is to be worshipped

EXO 20:2–20:3 parallel DEU 5:6–5:7 See also EXO 23:13, EXO 23:24; LEV 19:4; DEU 6:13–6:14; 2KI 17:35

Idolatry is empty and worthless

JON 2:8 See also DEU 4:28; ISA 44:18–44:20, ISA 45:20; HAB 2:18

title: 1170: God, unity of
content:
God acts as a unity in all his deeds. The three persons of the Trinity are united in one Godhead. They are interrelated with one another, share the same attributes and co-operate in the same work. God's characteristics are consistent, expressing his integral divine nature.

God is one

DEU 6:4 See also MRK 12:29, MRK 12:32; 1CO 8:4; GAL 3:20; EPH 4:5–4:6; 1TI 2:5; JAS 2:19

The three persons of the Trinity are united in one Godhead

All three are interrelated

ROM 8:9–8:11 The interchange between the persons of the Trinity reflects their unity in terms of activity. See also JHN 14:23; 2CO 13:14; EPH 2:18; 1PE 1:2

The Son is one with the Father

JHN 10:30 See also MAT 10:40; MRK 9:37 parallel LUK 9:48; LUK 10:16; JHN 8:16, JHN 14:9

The Spirit is one with God

2SA 23:2–23:3 See also PSA 51:11; 1CO 3:16

The three persons are one in character

All three are eternal

ROM 16:26 the Father; ; ISA 9:6 the Son; ; HEB 9:14 the Spirit

All three are all-knowing

ISA 40:13–40:14 the Father; JHN 2:24–2:25, JHN 21:17; 1CO 2:10–2:11 the Spirit

All three are holy

JHN 17:11 the Father; MRK 1:24; ; ACT 3:14; JHN 14:26; ; ROM 1:4

All three are called Lord and God

The Father: MAT 11:25; ; ACT 3:22; ; 1CO 8:6; JHN 1:1, JHN 20:28; ; ACT 10:36; ; 2PE 1:1; ACT 5:3–5:4; ; 2CO 3:17–3:18

All three are glorious

The Father: ACT 7:2; ; EPH 1:17; JHN 1:14, JHN 12:41; 2CO 3:8, 2CO 3:18; ; 1PE 4:14

The three persons share in the same work

In creation

GEN 1:1–1:3 See also PSA 33:6, PSA 104:30; JHN 1:3–1:4; COL 1:16

In Jesus Christ's ministry

JHN 3:34 See also ACT 10:38

In salvation

1PE 1:2 See also 2CO 1:21–1:22; TIT 3:4–3:6

In indwelling the church

EPH 2:22 1CO 3:16, 1CO 6:19; GAL 2:20; ; EPH 3:17

In directing the church's mission

ACT 16:6–16:10 ACT 14:27; ; GAL 1:1; MAT 28:18–28:20; ; ACT 1:7–1:8; ACT 13:2, ACT 13:4

God's characteristics are consistent

His love and justice are in unity

EXO 34:6–34:7 See also 2SA 7:14–7:15; PSA 103:6–103:8; ROM 3:25–3:26

His wrath and mercy are in unity

JOL 2:11–2:13 See also JHN 3:16–3:18 It is particularly in the cross of Jesus Christ that the perfect balance between God's wrath and justice and his love and mercy are seen; ROM 2:4, ROM 2:7–2:8

title: 1175: God, will of
content:
The intent and purpose of God, as revealed in Scripture. God's will for his creation and his people is set out in the Law and the Prophets, which find their fulfilment in Jesus Christ. A central aspect of the will of God is that his people be faithful and obedient.

The revelation of God's will

In his word

2TI 3:16 See also 2SA 7:21 parallel 1CH 17:19; PSA 103:20–103:21; COL 1:25–1:26; 1JN 2:4–2:5

Through the law

EXO 18:15–18:16, EXO 24:12; ; DEU 30:16; ; PSA 119:43; ; ROM 7:12; ; 1TI 1:8

Through the prophets

1KI 22:6–22:7 parallel 2CH 18:5–18:6; ; 2KI 3:11; ; JER 42:3–42:4; ; EZK 12:25; ; AMO 3:7

In the apostolic gospel

ACT 20:27; ; GAL 1:11–1:12

God's will is fulfilled in Jesus Christ

EPH 1:9–1:10 See also EPH 3:4–3:11; COL 1:27

Jesus Christ obeyed his Father's will

JHN 6:38 See also MAT 26:39 parallels MRK 14:36, LUK 22:42; JHN 4:34, JHN 5:30; HEB 10:7; PSA 40:8

God desires justice and righteousness

1TI 2:4 See also EZK 18:23, EZK 33:11; MAT 18:14; JHN 3:16–3:17; 2PE 3:9

God desires loving obedience

MAT 22:35–22:38 parallels MRK 12:28–12:29, LUK 10:27 See also DEU 6:5PSA 51:16–51:17; ISA 1:11; JER 7:22–7:23; 1JN 2:5

Rejecting God's will

title: 1185: God, zeal of
content:
The intense and protective commitment of God to his people and to his purposes which seeks a passionate and obedient response. The zeal of God is often linked with the idea of the “jealousy of God”, in which God refuses to contemplate his people giving their allegiance or affection to another.

title: 1190: glory
content: The distinctive feature of the presence of God, often compared to power, weight or brightness. Scripture affirms that God's glory is made known through his work of creation, his acts of intervention in history and supremely in the life and resurrection of Jesus Christ.

title: 1205: God, titles and names of
content:
These reflect the great and varied aspects of God's being and character. They are particularly used by the psalmists to speak of God as a source of strength, security, blessing and hope.

Titles which denote God's greatness

God Almighty

GEN 17:1 This was the special name by which God revealed himself to the patriarchs. The Hebrew is “El-Shaddai” possibly meaning “God the mountain”, emphasising God's might. See also GEN 28:3, GEN 48:3; EXO 6:3; RUT 1:20–1:21; JOB 5:17JOB 6:4, JOB 37:23 PSA 91:1; REV 16:14, REV 19:15

GEN 14:18–14:20 The Hebrew “El-Elyon” denotes the supremacy and sovereignty of God. See also GEN 14:22; DEU 32:8; 2SA 22:14 parallel PSA 18:13; PSA 9:2, PSA 21:7, PSA 46:4, PSA 47:2, PSA 78:35, PSA 97:9; ISA 14:14; DAN 3:26MRK 5:7 parallel LUK 8:28; LUK 1:32, LUK 1:35 LUK 6:35; ACT 16:17

title: 1225: God, as Spirit
content:
God has no material body and he is not subject to human limitations. While his spiritual nature is clearly inferred in Scripture, it is seldom directly stated; the emphasis is on his dynamic, living personhood.

title: 1230: God, the Lord
content:
God is the almighty ruler to whom everything and everyone is subject. His lordship is seen in his power and his victory over his enemies and also in his deliverance of his people and his loving care for them. He is the recipient of their obedience and their prayers. (“Lord” is the translation of the Hebrew word “Adonai” meaning “Ruler” or “Master” and is distinct from “LORD” which is the translation of the divine name “Yahweh”.).

God is the almighty ruler

He is Lord of lords

1TI 6:15 God exercises his lordship over all creation, but especially over all humanity. See also DEU 10:17; PSA 136:3; DAN 2:47
