title: 1235: God, the LORD
content:
The translation of the Hebrew name “Yahweh” which is the personal name of God whose meaning was revealed to Moses. It emphasises that God is the one who is eternal, unique, unchangeable and always actively present with his people. It expresses God's role as Israel's Redeemer and covenant Lord.

The name of God, the LORD

The LORD is the personal name of God

ISA 42:8 “The LORD” is the NIV rendering of the Hebrew “Yahweh”.

The name's meaning

EXO 3:14–3:15 This forms part of God's explanation of who he is. “I AM” (in Hebrew closely resembling “Yahweh”) expresses the ever-present, unchanging, totally dependable character of God.

The name is revealed to Moses

EXO 6:2–6:3 The name had been used previously (e.g., Ge 4:26) but its meaning was not fully understood until God revealed it to Moses in the context of the exodus from Egypt.

The name is glorious and awesome

DEU 28:58–28:59 See also JOS 7:9; 1SA 12:22

The name is not to be misused

EXO 20:7 parallel DEU 5:11 The misuse referred to here is swearing falsely by God's name. See also LEV 19:12, LEV 24:11, LEV 24:15–24:16 The Jews came to believe that just uttering the name “Yahweh” was an offence and as a result its exact pronunciation has been lost. What merited the death penalty here was using God's name as a curse.

God's character is revealed by his name the LORD

He is unique

EXO 8:10; ; DEU 4:35, DEU 4:39; ; 1KI 8:60; ; ISA 45:5, ISA 45:18; ; JOL 2:27

He is eternal

GEN 21:33; ; PSA 9:7, PSA 29:10, PSA 102:12, PSA 135:13; ; ISA 26:4

He is holy

LEV 11:44; ; 1SA 2:2; ; PSA 30:4; ; ISA 43:15

He is powerful

EXO 15:6; ; JOS 22:22; ; PSA 93:4; ; JER 10:6

He is majestic

PSA 83:18, PSA 93:1, PSA 97:9; ; ISA 2:10, ISA 2:19, ISA 2:21; ; MIC 5:4

He is compassionate and gracious

EXO 34:6–34:7; ; PSA 103:8, PSA 145:8–145:9

He is faithful and true

PSA 145:13

He is unchangeable

PSA 33:11, PSA 110:4; ; MAL 3:6

He is actively present with his people

DEU 31:6; ; JOS 1:9; ; 1KI 8:57; ; HAG 1:13

God reveals the relevance of his name the LORD by his actions

In creation

JER 33:2 See also ISA 42:5, ISA 43:15 “The LORD” is Creator of the universe but also the Creator of the people Israel; ISA 45:18 Human beings are the purpose of God's creation; they know God as “the LORD”.

In the deliverance of his people from Egypt

EXO 6:6 Deliverance is a central concept in the revelation of the divine name. See also EXO 12:51, EXO 14:30, EXO 16:6, EXO 20:2; LEV 26:13; PSA 81:10

In establishing a covenant with his people

EXO 6:7 See also EXO 29:46; 2SA 7:24 parallel 1CH 17:22; JER 14:9

In the judgment of his enemies

EXO 12:12 See also EXO 7:5, EXO 8:22–8:23; 1SA 17:45–17:47; 2CH 14:11–14:13

In the restoration of his people

EZK 36:36 See also PSA 102:15–102:22; ISA 60:15–60:22; EZK 37:6, EZK 37:13–37:14 The theme “then you, my people, will know that I am the LORD” (with slight variations) runs throughout the book of Ezekiel and is connected to divine acts of judgment and restoration.

God, the LORD denotes God's lordship

He is the Sovereign LORD

EZK 2:4 “Sovereign LORD” is the NIV rendering of “Adonai Yahweh”, the favourite divine title in Ezekiel. See also GEN 15:2, GEN 15:8; DEU 3:24; JOS 7:7; JDG 6:22; 2SA 7:18–7:29; PSA 73:28; ISA 61:1; JER 1:6; AMO 1:8

He is the LORD Almighty

1SA 1:3 “The LORD Almighty” is the NIV rendering of “Yahweh Sabaoth” (“the LORD of hosts”) which emphasises the sovereignty of “the LORD”. See also 1CH 11:9; PSA 24:10, PSA 46:7, PSA 46:11; ISA 2:12; JER 39:16; HAG 2:6–2:9; ZEC 1:3–1:4, ZEC 1:6; MAL 1:14

He is the Lord, the LORD Almighty

ISA 1:24 “The Lord, the LORD Almighty” is the NIV rendering of “Adonai Yahweh Sabaoth” which is often associated with the LORD's authority to rule and to judge. See also PSA 69:6; ISA 3:15, ISA 19:4; JER 2:19, JER 46:10, JER 49:5; AMO 9:5

As the LORD, God interacts with his people

He enforces the moral code

LEV 18:1–18:5 The phrase “I am the LORD” occurs over 40 times in Leviticus chapters 18–26 and is the authority behind the moral law given to God's people. See also LEV 11:44–11:45, LEV 19:2

He makes his people holy

LEV 22:32 See also EXO 31:13; LEV 20:8, LEV 21:8; EZK 20:12, EZK 37:28

He is the object of his people's worship

EXO 4:22–4:23 The purpose of Israel's redemption from Egypt was that they would be free to worship the LORD. See also EXO 12:31, EXO 23:25; 1CH 16:29 parallel PSA 96:9; PSA 29:2, PSA 99:5, PSA 100:2; JON 1:9

Jesus Christ takes God's name to himself

JHN 8:58–8:59 The form of expression used by Jesus Christ meant that he was clearly identifying himself with the divine name “I AM”. The response of the crowd confirms this.

title: 1240: God, the Rock
content:
An OT title for God and a Messianic title signifying that God's people can rely on him for absolute protection and salvation.

Rock as a title for Israel's God

PSA 78:35 See also GEN 49:24; DEU 32:15, DEU 32:18, DEU 32:30; 2SA 23:3; PSA 42:9; ISA 30:29; HAB 1:12

God the Rock is unique

2SA 22:32 parallel PSA 18:31 See also 1SA 2:2; ISA 44:8

The Rock is superior to other gods

DEU 32:31 See also DEU 32:37

The Rock is worthy to be praised

PSA 144:1 See also DEU 32:4; PSA 92:15

God the Rock and his people

God the Rock is a refuge for his people

PSA 62:7 See also PSA 28:1, PSA 31:1–31:3, PSA 61:2, PSA 71:3; ISA 26:4

God the Rock is his people's fortress

PSA 94:22 See also PSA 28:8, PSA 46:7, PSA 46:11, PSA 48:3, PSA 59:9, PSA 59:16–59:17, PSA 91:2, PSA 144:2; JER 16:19

God the Rock is his people's security

2SA 22:3 parallel PSA 18:2 See also PSA 9:9, PSA 27:1, PSA 37:39, PSA 43:2, PSA 52:7; JOL 3:16

God the Rock saves and delivers his people

PSA 95:1 See also 2SA 22:47 parallel PSA 18:46; PSA 19:14, PSA 62:2, PSA 89:26; ISA 17:10

Rock as a Messianic title

The Messiah is the rock/stone on which God's living temple stands

ISA 8:14, ISA 28:16 See also PSA 118:22; MAT 21:42 parallels MRK 12:10, LUK 20:17; ACT 4:11; 1PE 2:6–2:7

The Messiah's kingdom is eternal and immovable like a rock

DAN 2:34–2:35 See also DAN 2:44–2:45

Consequences of rejecting the rock/stone

MAT 21:44 parallel LUK 20:18; ; ROM 9:32–9:33; ; 1PE 2:4–2:8

resource.label: 2203: Jesus Christ, titles and names of

title: 1245: God of the fathers
content:
God's particular association with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, the fathers of the nations of Israel, highlights his faithfulness in fulfilling the promises made to them. He is the God who establishes a covenant with his people for their everlasting blessing.

God's relationship with the fathers

The God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob

ACT 7:32 See also EXO 3:6, EXO 3:15, EXO 4:5

The God of Jacob

PSA 20:1 “Jacob” is used here as a synonym for the nation of Israel. This usage is common in the Psalms as a reminder to God's people of his faithfulness to Jacob and the emergent nation of Israel. See also GEN 32:27–32:28; PSA 14:7, PSA 46:7, PSA 46:11, PSA 75:9, PSA 76:6, PSA 114:7

Such a relationship served as an encouragement to others

Abraham's servant was encouraged to trust God in his mission by the example of Abraham's faith: GEN 24:12, GEN 24:27, GEN 24:42, GEN 24:48; GEN 26:24, GEN 31:42, GEN 31:53; GEN 46:1–46:3 Jacob learned to fear and trust God through his father Isaac.

God's covenant with the fathers

God's promises that they will be the fathers of a great nation

GEN 28:13–28:15 See also GEN 26:2–26:4; EXO 3:16–3:17

God remembers and fulfils his promises

EXO 2:24 See also EXO 6:3–6:5, EXO 32:13; LEV 26:42; DEU 1:8; ACT 7:17

God makes a covenant with his people to be their God

DEU 29:12–29:13 See also GEN 17:7; EXO 6:7–6:8; PSA 105:8–105:11

Characteristics of the God of the fathers

He blesses his people

DEU 1:11 See also DEU 30:20

He is powerful

1KI 18:36–18:39

He is gracious and compassionate

2KI 13:23

He keeps his people faithful

1CH 29:18

He gives wisdom

DAN 2:23

He is the God of the living

MAT 22:32 parallels MRK 12:26–12:27, LUK 20:37–20:38

People sometimes fail to respond to the God of their fathers

JOS 18:3 See also NUM 32:11; DEU 6:10–6:12, DEU 9:27; 2CH 30:7

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 1250: Abba
content:
The Aramaic word “Abba” is thought to be a very intimate term for “Father” suggesting that those who use it to refer to God enjoy a close relationship with him. Jesus Christ uses the term as a consequence of his natural sonship of God; believers may use it as a consequence of their adopted sonship of God through faith.

``Abba'' used in addressing God

By Jesus Christ

MRK 14:36

By believers

ROM 8:15; ; GAL 4:6

title: 1255: face of God
content:
A term used to refer to the character of God, especially his favour towards his people. For God to turn his face to his people is to offer them his grace and help; for God to turn his face against his people is to withhold his favour and blessing. To seek the face of God is to seek his favour.

The face of God as a sign of blessing

NUM 6:25–6:26 See also 2SA 21:1; 1CH 16:11; PSA 4:6, PSA 31:16, PSA 67:1, PSA 80:19, PSA 105:4, PSA 119:135; EZK 39:29

Parts of God's face

His ears

2SA 22:7; ; 2KI 19:28; ; PSA 34:15, PSA 102:2; ; ISA 59:1

His eyes

PSA 34:15 See also DEU 11:12; 2CH 16:9; PSA 139:16; PRO 15:3

His hair

DAN 7:9

His lips

PSA 89:34; ; ISA 11:4, ISA 30:27

His mouth

2SA 22:9; ; JOB 15:30; ; ISA 45:23

His nostrils

2SA 22:9, 2SA 22:16

The visibility of God's face

His face cannot be seen

EXO 33:20–33:23 “The face of God” may mean here God's real and true self, the inference being that no-one can look on God as he is in his total purity and majesty.

Apparent exceptions where God is seen face to face

GEN 32:30 After Jacob's wrestling with a figure whose exact identity is not explicit in the preceding verses. Jacob did experience a real encounter with God but God was in a real and approachable form, not in his transcendent and visible glory. See also EXO 33:11; NUM 12:8; DEU 5:4, DEU 34:10; EZK 20:35

The angels in heaven look upon the face of God

MAT 18:10

God must reveal himself if people are to see his face

JOB 34:29 The Greek word for “revelation” literally means “removing a veil from one's face”.

The righteous will see God's face in the life to come

1CO 13:12; ; REV 22:4

When God hides his face blessing is withheld

Complaints at God hiding his face

PSA 44:24 See also PSA 13:1, PSA 88:14

Cries for help

PSA 27:9 See also PSA 69:17, PSA 102:2, PSA 143:7

Statements concerning God's anger and judgment

ISA 54:8 See also DEU 31:17–31:18; PSA 22:24, PSA 30:7, PSA 51:9, PSA 104:29; ISA 8:17, ISA 64:7; JER 33:5; EZK 39:23–39:24; MIC 3:4; REV 6:16

Other phrases synonymous with God hiding his face

2CH 30:9; ; JER 18:17; ; EZK 7:22

God sets his face against sinners

LEV 17:10, LEV 20:3, LEV 26:17; ; PSA 34:16; ; EZK 14:8; ; 1PE 3:12; ; EZK 15:7

God's face as a symbol of his favour

Seeking the face of God in prayer

2CH 7:14 See also 2SA 21:1; 1CH 16:11; PSA 24:6, PSA 105:4, PSA 119:58; HOS 5:15

Seeking the favour of God

EXO 32:11 See also 1SA 13:12; 2KI 13:4; 2CH 33:12; JER 26:19; DAN 9:13; ZEC 7:2, ZEC 8:21; MAL 1:9

Appearing before God in worship

EXO 23:15 The phrase seems to recall the fact that in ancient religions worshippers came before carved faces of their idols. In Israel such physical representations of God were forbidden but the phrase was still used underlining the belief that Israel's God was present in his sanctuary. See also EXO 23:17, EXO 34:20; DEU 16:16, DEU 31:11; PSA 42:2; ISA 1:12

The shining of God's face upon his people indicates his blessing

NUM 6:25–6:26 See also PSA 4:6, PSA 31:16, PSA 44:3, PSA 67:1, PSA 80:3, PSA 80:19, PSA 119:135

God's face accompanies Israel as a symbol of his presence

EXO 33:14 Literally “my face will go ...” Here this phrase may allude not only to God's presence but also to the manifestations of his presence (e.g., the cloud in verse 9). See also DEU 4:37; ISA 63:9

title: 1260: finger of God
content:
A figure of speech which gives expression to God's creative power and authority over his creation.

God creates with his finger

PSA 8:3 See also ISA 48:13, ISA 64:8

God writes with his finger

He writes the law

EXO 31:18 See also EXO 24:12, EXO 32:16, EXO 34:1

He writes judgment

DAN 5:5 See also DAN 5:24–5:28

God works miracles with his finger

He sends plagues

EXO 8:19

He exorcises evil spirits

LUK 11:20

title: 1265: hand of God
content:
A figure of speech which points to God's sovereign power in creation and in his actions on his people's behalf, especially in redemption. Also used as a symbol of authority and in taking oaths.

God creates by his hand

ACT 7:50 See also ISA 66:2; JOB 10:3, JOB 10:8; PSA 8:3, PSA 19:1, PSA 95:5, PSA 138:8; ISA 48:13, ISA 64:8

God holds the world in his hand

PSA 95:4 See also JOB 12:10, JOB 26:13, JOB 36:32; PSA 31:15; ISA 40:12, ISA 41:19–41:20; DAN 5:23

God's creatures are fed from his hand

PSA 145:16 See also PSA 104:28; ISA 34:17

God's hand symbolises his power

God's hand is mighty

HAB 3:4 See also 1CH 29:12; PSA 118:15–118:16; JER 18:6; DAN 4:35; AMO 9:2

God executes judgment by the power of his hand

AMO 1:6–1:8 Judgment on the Philistine cities. See also DEU 32:41; 1SA 5:6–5:9; ISA 40:2; JER 15:6; LAM 2:3; EZK 6:14; ZEP 1:4; ACT 13:11

God's hand holds a cup of judgment

JER 25:15–25:17 See also PSA 75:8; ISA 51:17; HAB 2:16

God's hand brings trouble

JOB 19:21 See also RUT 1:13; 1SA 6:3; JOB 1:11; PSA 38:2, PSA 39:10

God's hand causes fear

ISA 19:16 See also ISA 23:11

Pleas for God to use his powerful hand

PSA 74:11 See also PSA 10:12, PSA 60:5 parallel PSA 108:6; PSA 77:10, PSA 144:7–144:8

God's powerful hand used on his people's behalf

God redeems by his powerful hand

PSA 138:7 See also 1KI 8:41–8:42 parallel 2CH 6:32; PSA 98:1; ISA 11:11; EZK 20:21–20:22, EZK 20:33–20:34; MIC 4:10

God redeemed Israel from Egypt by his powerful hand

EXO 13:3 See also EXO 3:19–3:20, EXO 13:9, EXO 13:14; DEU 5:15, DEU 7:8; NEH 1:10; JER 32:21; DAN 9:15

God's hand ensures success for his people

ISA 41:10 “righteous” has the sense of “victorious”. See also PSA 16:8, PSA 18:35, PSA 89:21; ISA 42:6, ISA 49:4

God's powerful hand is invincible

JHN 10:29 See also PSA 139:10; ISA 43:13

God's hand protects his people

EZR 8:31 See also EXO 33:22–33:23; 1CH 4:10; PSA 121:5; ISA 41:13, ISA 49:2, ISA 49:16, ISA 62:3

God's hand inspires the prophets

JER 1:9 See also ISA 8:11; JER 15:17; EZK 1:3, EZK 3:14, EZK 3:22, EZK 33:22, EZK 37:1, EZK 40:1

God's hand fulfils his purposes

EZR 7:6 See also 2CH 30:12; EZR 7:9, EZR 7:28, EZR 8:18; NEH 2:8, NEH 2:18; PRO 21:1; ECC 2:24; ISA 45:1; LUK 1:66; ACT 11:21

God's uplifted hand

Signifies his taking an oath

NEH 9:15 See also EXO 6:8; NUM 14:30; DEU 32:40; EZK 47:14

Signifies his power

ISA 9:12, ISA 9:17, ISA 9:21, ISA 26:11

God's right hand symbolises his authority

ACT 7:55–7:56 See also PSA 110:1; ACT 5:31; ROM 8:34; COL 3:1; HEB 1:3; 1PE 3:22

God's hand must be recognised

God requires submission under his hand

1PE 5:6 See also ROM 10:21; ISA 65:2

The works of God's hand must be acknowledged

PSA 28:5, PSA 92:4, PSA 143:5

title: 1270: right hand of God
content:
A figure of speech that represents God's ultimate power and authority, and where the exalted Jesus Christ now sits.

The right hand of God as a symbol

A symbol of power

PSA 89:13

A symbol of protection

PSA 16:8 See also PSA 63:8, PSA 139:10; ISA 41:13; LAM 2:3

A symbol of God's presence

PSA 16:11 See also PSA 48:10, PSA 73:23; ISA 62:8

A symbol of God's saving power

PSA 60:5 parallel PSA 108:6 See also PSA 17:7, PSA 20:6, PSA 80:15, PSA 98:1, PSA 138:7

A symbol of victory

PSA 18:35, PSA 44:3, PSA 78:54; ; ISA 41:10

A symbol of the defeat of God's enemies

EXO 15:6 See also EXO 15:12; PSA 21:8, PSA 74:11

A symbol of God's mighty works

PSA 45:4 See also PSA 77:10, PSA 118:15–118:16; ISA 48:13

A symbol of judgment

LAM 2:4; ; HAB 2:16

Jesus Christ at God's right hand

Jesus Christ is exalted to God's right hand

HEB 1:13 Comparison between Jesus Christ and the angels confirms Christ's absolute supremacy. See also PSA 110:1; MAT 22:44 parallels MRK 12:36, LUK 20:42; LUK 22:69; ACT 2:34, ACT 7:55–7:56; COL 3:1; HEB 1:3, HEB 12:2

Jesus Christ rules every authority at God's right hand

1PE 3:22 See also EPH 1:20–1:21

Jesus Christ continues his work at God's right hand

ROM 8:34 See also ACT 2:33, ACT 5:31; HEB 4:14–4:16, HEB 8:1

Jesus Christ will return at God's right hand

MAT 26:64 parallel MRK 14:62

Jesus Christ takes a scroll from God's right hand

REV 5:1–5:7 The scroll probably refers to God's decrees for the destiny of the world.

The right hand of Jesus Christ

The place of the righteous on judgment day

MAT 25:32–25:34

A privileged place granted by the Father

MAT 20:20–20:23 parallel MRK 10:35–10:40

A place of protection for Christians

REV 1:20 The angels of the churches are probably the church leaders. See also REV 1:16, REV 2:1

title: 1300: God, work of
content:
• 1305: God, activity of
  • 1335: blessing
  • 1340: consistency
  • 1345: covenant
  • 1355: providence

title: 1305: God, activity of
content:
God is not passive or remote from his creation but dynamically involved in all that he has made. In the past, this activity was seen in the history of Israel and in the ministry of Jesus Christ. In the present, God's activity can be seen in the life of believers and of the church.

God is active by nature

He actively works out his purposes

JHN 5:17 See also NUM 23:19; PSA 115:3; PRO 16:4; ISA 43:13

His names reflect his activity and involvement in his people's lives

GEN 22:14 “The LORD Will Provide”; ; EXO 15:26 “The LORD, who heals you”; ; EXO 17:15 “The LORD is my Banner”: this title emphasises that God is like a warrior who champions his people's cause; ; PSA 95:6 “The LORD our Maker”: the Hebrew word used does not refer to original creation but to the way in which God is fashioning a people for himself.

His word is active

ISA 55:10–55:11 The Hebrew for “word” can also be translated “deed” or “action”. God's word is not merely a communication of abstract truth but a powerful force active in the world. See also GEN 1:3; EZK 37:4, EZK 37:7

God is active in the creation

In making the universe

NEH 9:6 See also GEN 1:1, GEN 1:31, GEN 2:2, GEN 5:1–5:2; PSA 102:25; AMO 4:13; ACT 17:24; REV 4:11

In sustaining the created order

PSA 103:19 See also PSA 104:10–104:17, PSA 107:33–107:35; MAT 6:28–6:30; ACT 14:17; EPH 1:11; COL 1:16–1:17

In bringing about changes in the weather

JOB 37:10, JOB 38:22–38:30, JOB 38:34–38:35; ; PSA 107:25, PSA 147:8; ; MAT 5:45; ; ACT 14:17

In the stars and other heavenly bodies

JOB 38:31–38:33; ; PSA 104:19, PSA 147:4; ; ISA 40:26; ; JAS 1:17

In the animal creation

JOB 39:1–39:30, JOB 40:15–40:24, JOB 41:1–41:11; ; PSA 104:20–104:27, PSA 147:9; ; MAT 10:29 parallel LUK 12:6

God is active in Israel's history

In the calling and creation of Israel as a people

EXO 6:6–6:8 See also GEN 12:1–12:3; EXO 19:3–19:6; DEU 4:32–4:34; ISA 51:2; EZK 16:6–16:7; HOS 11:1–11:4; MAL 2:10

In saving and delivering his people

PSA 106:2 This verse introduces a psalm which recounts the history of God's dealings with his people. See also EXO 33:16–33:17; 2SA 7:7–7:11; 1KI 18:36–18:38; PSA 28:8–28:9, PSA 107:2–107:3

In judgment

NUM 25:3–25:4; ; 1SA 2:25, 1SA 12:16–12:18; ; 2SA 21:1

In bringing victory in battle

EXO 14:27–14:28, EXO 17:8–17:16; ; NUM 21:1–21:3; ; JOS 6:2, JOS 10:30; ; JDG 7:9, JDG 7:22; ; 1SA 17:47; ; 2CH 20:22–20:23

In raising up and sending leaders to the people

2SA 7:8–7:9; ; 2CH 24:19; ; EST 4:14; ; JER 7:25; ; ZEC 7:7

God is active in the nations of the world

His rule over the nations

PSA 22:28 See also GEN 11:8; DEU 32:8; JDG 2:20–2:23; JOB 12:23; PSA 46:8–46:10, PSA 66:7; ISA 41:2; ACT 17:26

Examples of nations through which God acts

Assyria: 2KI 17:18–17:23; ; ISA 28:11; 2KI 24:10–24:14; ; 2CH 36:15–36:18; 2CH 36:22–36:23 parallel EZR 1:1–1:4 Persia

God is active in human life

He determines the course of human lives

ACT 17:28 See also 1CH 29:12; PSA 8:3–8:4, PSA 75:6–75:7, PSA 139:16; LUK 1:52; JHN 1:4

Examples of God's intervention in human lives

NUM 22:21–22:23 Balaam; ; 1SA 1:19–1:20 Hannah; ; 2KI 5:14–5:15 Naaman; ; JON 1:17 Jonah; ACT 9:3–9:6, ACT 13:1–13:3, ACT 16:6, ACT 16:10

God is active in Jesus Christ's ministry

ACT 10:38 See also LUK 5:17, LUK 11:20; JHN 3:2, JHN 5:17–5:19, JHN 9:3–9:4, JHN 14:10; 2CO 5:19

God is active through the Holy Spirit

GEN 1:2; ; 2KI 2:16; ; EZK 8:3, EZK 11:1, EZK 43:5

God is active in the church

1CO 12:6 This verse comes in the context of teaching about the body of Christ and the gifts which the Spirit gives to the church. See also 1CO 14:24–14:25; GAL 2:8, GAL 3:5; EPH 3:10, EPH 3:20–3:21; PHP 1:6, PHP 2:13

title: 1310: God, as judge
content:
As a part of his sovereignty and authority, God is executor of his righteousness within the created order. Jesus Christ shares in this ongoing work.

God has authority to judge

God's status as judge

PSA 75:7 See also PSA 50:6, PSA 76:8–76:9; ECC 11:9; ISA 33:22, ISA 66:16; 2TI 4:8; HEB 12:23; JAS 4:12

He decides disputes

JDG 11:27 See also GEN 16:5, GEN 31:53; 1SA 24:15; ISA 2:4 parallel MIC 4:3; JAS 5:9

He presides in the heavenly court

ISA 3:13 See also PSA 50:4, PSA 82:1; DAN 7:9–7:10; JOL 3:12; REV 20:11–20:15

God is judge over the whole of creation

He judges the earth

GEN 18:25 See also PSA 9:8, PSA 58:11, PSA 82:8, PSA 94:2, PSA 96:13, PSA 98:9

He judges every individual

EZK 33:20 See also ECC 3:17; HEB 9:27; 1PE 4:5; JUD 1:15; REV 20:12

He judges the nations

JOL 3:12 See also PSA 9:19–9:20, PSA 110:6; OBA 1:15; ZEP 3:8

He judges rulers of nations

ISA 40:23; ; JER 25:17–25:27; ; REV 6:15–6:17

He judges his own people

HEB 10:30 See also DEU 32:36; PSA 78:62; JER 1:16; 1PE 4:17

He judges angels

2PE 2:4; ; JUD 1:6

He judges Satan

GEN 3:14–3:15; ; MAT 25:41; ; 1TI 3:6; ; REV 20:10

God's judgment is inescapable

No-one can hide from God

OBA 1:4 See also GEN 3:8–3:9; JOB 11:20; JER 11:11; AMO 9:1–9:4

God searches human hearts

JER 17:10 See also 1CH 28:9; PSA 7:9; PRO 5:21; JER 11:20

God reveals secrets

ROM 2:16 See also ECC 12:14; JER 16:17; 1CO 4:5; HEB 4:13

Examples of God acting as judge

His judgment of the earth in sending the flood

GEN 6:7, GEN 6:13, GEN 6:17, GEN 7:21–7:23

His judgment of individuals

GEN 4:9–4:12 Cain; ; ACT 5:3–5:10 Ananias and Sapphira; ; ACT 13:8–13:11 Elymas the sorcerer

His judgment of families

JOS 7:24–7:25 of Achan; ; 1SA 3:12–3:13 of Eli

His judgment of cities

GEN 19:24–19:25 Sodom and Gomorrah; ; JOS 6:24 Jericho

His judgment of nations

DEU 7:1–7:5 the Canaanite nations

His judgment of the rulers of nations

2CH 26:16–26:21 Uzziah; ; DAN 4:31–4:33 Nebuchadnezzar; ; DAN 5:22–5:30 Belshazzar; ; ACT 12:22–12:23 Herod

His judgment of his own people

JDG 2:11–2:15; ; 2CH 36:15–36:20; ; ISA 33:22

God's character is revealed through his righteous judgments

His sovereignty

PSA 9:7, PSA 96:10, PSA 99:4; ; EZK 6:14

His power

EXO 6:6, EXO 14:31; ; EZK 20:33–20:36; ; REV 18:8

His holiness

LEV 10:1–10:3; ; 1SA 6:19–6:20; ; EZK 28:22; ; REV 16:5

His anger

NAM 1:2–1:3; ; ROM 2:5

His truth

PSA 96:13; ; ROM 2:2; ; REV 16:7

His impartiality

2CH 19:7; ; ROM 2:9–2:11; ; COL 3:25; ; 1PE 1:17

His compassion

LAM 3:31–3:33; ; HOS 11:8–11:9; ; JON 3:10, JON 4:2

His patience

NUM 14:18; ; NEH 9:30; ; 2PE 3:9

His mercy

NEH 9:31; ; JOB 9:15; ; PSA 78:38; ; MIC 7:18

Jesus Christ continues God's work as judge

JHN 5:22 See also JHN 5:27; ACT 10:42

God will act as judge on the last day

REV 20:12 See also ISA 2:17; ZEP 1:14–1:18; JHN 12:48; ACT 17:31; ROM 2:16, ROM 14:10; 1CO 4:5; 2TI 4:1; 1PE 4:5; 2PE 2:9, 2PE 3:7

title: 1315: God, as redeemer
content:
God alone has the ability to save his people from slavery and captivity. God's redemptive will and power is demonstrated in his deliverance of Israel from bondage in Egypt, and supremely through the death and resurrection of Jesus Christ.

God as the sole redeemer of Israel

ISA 47:4 See also DEU 13:5; PSA 19:14

He redeems because of his love

DEU 7:8; ; PSA 44:26

He is able to redeem because of his power

DEU 9:26; ; 2SA 7:23 parallel 1CH 17:21; ; NEH 1:10

God acts as Israel's redeemer

PSA 78:35, PSA 111:9; ; ISA 43:1, ISA 48:17, ISA 54:5, ISA 63:16

He promises to redeem Israel from Egypt

EXO 3:8–3:10 See also EXO 3:21, EXO 14:4

He redeems Israel in the exodus from Egypt

DEU 5:15 See also DEU 15:15, DEU 21:8, DEU 24:18; NEH 9:9–9:11; PSA 77:14–77:20, PSA 105:23–105:38; HOS 13:4; MIC 6:4; ACT 7:30–7:36

He redeems Israel from Babylonian exile

ISA 41:14 “Redeemer” is a characteristic title for God in the second part of Isaiah. In the context of the exile Isaiah reminds Israel that God would act as redeemer. See also ISA 43:4, ISA 44:6, ISA 44:24, ISA 49:26, ISA 54:5, ISA 60:16, ISA 62:12; JER 50:33–50:35, JER 51:12; MIC 4:10; ZEC 10:8

God redeems his people from difficult personal circumstances

PSA 34:22 See also ISA 29:22 Abraham was redeemed from the land of idolatry to become God's servant; JOB 19:25 Job, in the midst of his despair, declares God to be his redeemer; PSA 31:4–31:5 part of the psalmist's prayer for deliverance when confronted by an evil conspiracy; LAM 3:58 the lamentor recognises personal redemption within the context of national despair

He brings deliverance from enemies

PSA 69:18, PSA 106:10, PSA 107:2; ; JER 15:21, JER 31:11

God redeems his people from the bondage and guilt of sin

PSA 130:7–130:8 See also PSA 34:22; ISA 59:20

Those who remain sinful do not find redemption

HOS 7:13 See also PSA 26:11 The request for redemption is made on the basis of uprightness; ISA 1:27–1:28

God redeems his people from death

PSA 49:15 See also JOB 19:25–19:27; PSA 103:4

Jesus Christ is central to God's redemptive purposes

COL 1:13–1:14 See also LUK 1:68; ROM 3:23–3:24; 1CO 1:30, 1CO 7:23; GAL 3:13–3:14, GAL 4:4–4:5; EPH 1:7; HEB 9:11–9:12; 1PE 1:18–1:21

God will finally redeem all creation at Jesus Christ's second coming

ROM 8:19–8:23 See also LUK 21:28; EPH 1:13–1:14, EPH 4:30; TIT 2:13–2:14

God's redemptive work brings him praise and worship

EPH 1:3–1:7 See also EXO 15:1–15:21; PSA 71:23, PSA 111:9, PSA 119:134, PSA 136:1–136:26; ISA 35:4–35:10, ISA 44:22–44:23, ISA 48:20, ISA 51:11, ISA 52:9

title: 1320: God, as Saviour
content:
God is the only Saviour and deliverer of his people. His work of salvation is inaugurated in the OT, and reaches its climax in the work of Jesus Christ.

God is the only Saviour

ISA 45:21–45:22 See also JDG 10:13–10:14; ISA 43:11; JER 2:28, JER 3:23, JER 11:12

God's deliverance can be worked through a human agent

JDG 2:16 See also GEN 45:5–45:7; EXO 3:10; JOS 10:6–10:10; JDG 2:18, JDG 6:14–6:16; 2KI 13:5; ISA 45:1, ISA 45:13

Examples of God's saving deeds

God delivers his people from Egypt: EXO 6:6–6:7, EXO 14:13–14:14, EXO 14:21–14:23, EXO 14:27–14:28; ; PSA 77:19–77:20; ; HOS 13:4; 1SA 17:37; ; 2SA 22:1–22:3 parallel PSA 18:2; ; 2SA 22:18 parallel PSA 18:17; ; PSA 3:8; DAN 6:16–6:22 God delivers Daniel from the lions' den.; ACT 5:18–5:19, ACT 12:6–12:11, ACT 16:25–16:26

God's acts of deliverance reveal his nature and character

ISA 52:10 See also PSA 98:2; ISA 49:6; LUK 3:4–3:6; ISA 40:3–40:5; ROM 1:16

His strength is seen

PSA 18:2 “rock” and “horn” symbolise God's strength; they are fequently used to emphasise his ability to save. See also EXO 15:2 parallel PSA 118:14; 1SA 2:1; 2SA 22:2–22:3, 2SA 22:47 parallel PSA 18:46; PSA 28:8

His glory is seen

EXO 14:4 See also EXO 14:17–14:18; NUM 14:22; PSA 85:9; ISA 66:19; EZK 39:21

God's deliverance is anticipated

PSA 31:2 See also GEN 49:18; PSA 35:4, PSA 69:13–69:15; ISA 33:22, ISA 35:4

God's deliverance is promised

EXO 6:6 See also PSA 50:15, PSA 91:14; ISA 38:6, ISA 46:4; EZK 34:12

Implications of God's deliverance for believers

God is able to deliver believers from trials and testing

2TI 4:18 See also DAN 12:1; MAT 6:13; 1CO 10:13; 2PE 2:9; REV 3:10

God is able to deliver believers from sin and death

ROM 7:24–7:25 God delivers both from death owing to adversity and from death that comes about as a result of sin. See also 2SA 12:13; PSA 33:19; EZK 37:23; 1CO 15:56–15:57; 2CO 1:9–1:10

Testimony to God as Saviour and deliverer

GEN 50:20 See also PSA 13:5, PSA 18:46–18:48; ISA 61:10; DAN 6:27; HAB 3:18; LUK 1:47; 1TI 4:10

title: 1325: God, the Creator
content:
God's power is revealed in the initial creation and the continuing sustenance of the universe. Humanity represents the climax of the Creator's purposes.

God is the Creator

NEH 9:6 See also GEN 1:1; ISA 45:7, ISA 66:2; EPH 3:9; REV 4:11

God's creative activity demonstrates his uniqueness

PSA 96:5 See also ISA 37:16; JER 10:11

God's creative activity implies that he is good

1TI 4:4 See also GEN 1:4, GEN 1:31

God creates all things

He creates the physical world

ISA 45:18 See also GEN 1:1–1:25

He creates the spiritual world

PSA 148:2–148:5 See also ROM 8:38–8:39; COL 1:16

The human race is the apex of God's creation

PSA 8:3–8:8 See also GEN 1:26–1:28, GEN 5:1, GEN 9:6; PSA 100:3, PSA 139:13; ISA 42:5; ACT 17:26–17:28

The Creator's agents

He creates through Jesus Christ

1CO 8:6 See also JHN 1:3; COL 1:16; HEB 1:2

He creates by the Spirit

JOB 33:4 See also GEN 1:2; PSA 104:30

He creates by his word

PSA 33:6 See also JOB 38:8–38:11; PSA 33:9

He creates by wisdom

PRO 3:19 Wisdom is personified and is active with the Creator. See also PRO 8:30; JER 10:12–10:13

He creates from nothing

HEB 11:3 God creates out of non-existent material, which emphasises the absolute dependence of all creation upon him. See also JOB 26:7; ROM 4:17

The Creator sustains the created order

ISA 44:24 The Hebrew indicates continuous activity. See also DEU 30:20; JOB 9:8–9:9; PSA 104:2–104:4; ISA 42:5

Examples of God sustaining creation

MAT 5:45 See also JOB 5:10, JOB 38:31–38:33; MAT 6:28–6:30

The Creator watches over his creation

PSA 33:14 See also PSA 121:2–121:8

The creation is a vehicle for God's self-revelation

ROM 1:20 See also PSA 19:1; ISA 40:26; AMO 4:13

God renews his creation through redemption

JHN 3:17; 2CO 5:17 See also JHN 1:12–1:13; ROM 4:17, ROM 6:4, ROM 8:19–8:23 The redemption of the physical world must wait until the sons of God are revealed at the end of time; GAL 6:15

God renews his new creation of believing people

2CO 4:16 See also COL 3:10

God will reveal a new universe

REV 21:1 See also REV 21:5; ISA 65:17

title: 1330: God, the provider
content:
God supplies the needs of all creation, but gives special care to his own people.

God is called ``The LORD who provides''

GEN 22:13–22:14 The name “Yahweh Yireh” (or “Jehovah Jireh”) is from the verb “to see”, and means “The LORD foresees” or “The LORD will see to it”. See also GEN 22:8

God provides for the needs of all creation

He provides for the earth

PSA 65:9–65:13 See also DEU 11:12, DEU 11:14–11:15; PSA 68:9, PSA 104:10–104:18, PSA 135:7, PSA 147:8; EZK 34:26–34:29

He provides for the animals

PSA 145:16 See also JOB 38:41; PSA 104:27–104:28, PSA 147:9; MAT 6:26 parallel LUK 12:24

He provides for all people

ACT 14:17 See also 1CH 29:12; PSA 68:5, PSA 107:9; MAT 5:45; ACT 17:28

He provides for the poor and needy

PSA 140:12 See also 1SA 2:8; PSA 35:10, PSA 145:13–145:14, PSA 146:7–146:9; ISA 25:4

Special instances of God's practical provision

Food and water in the wilderness: EXO 16:13–16:18, EXO 17:3–17:6; ; NUM 11:4–11:9, NUM 11:31–11:32; 1KI 17:6, 1KI 17:16, 1KI 19:5–19:8; 2KI 4:42–4:44 Elisha feeds 100 men from 20 barley loaves; ; MAT 14:15–14:21 parallels MRK 6:35–6:44, LUK 9:12–9:17, JHN 6:5–6:13 Jesus Christ feeds 5,000; ; MAT 15:32–15:38 parallel MRK 8:1–8:9 Jesus Christ feeds 4,000

God's special provision for his own people

He provides for their practical needs

PHP 4:19 See also DEU 2:7; PSA 68:10, PSA 111:5; MAT 6:25–6:33 parallel LUK 12:22–12:31

He provides for their protection

PSA 5:12 See also GEN 28:15; DEU 33:27; 1SA 2:9; PSA 91:9–91:13, PSA 121:3–121:8; ISA 46:4; PHP 4:7; 1PE 1:5; JUD 1:24; REV 3:10

He provides for their every need

2PE 1:3 See also 1CO 3:21–3:23; COL 2:10

Examples of God's provision for his people

2SA 7:10 parallel 1CH 17:9 security in the promised land; ; ISA 61:3 consolation in grief; ; JER 33:6–33:9 restoration from captivity; ; 1CO 10:13 a way of escape from temptation

Implications of God's providential care

Freedom from worry

MAT 6:31–6:33 parallel LUK 12:29–12:31

Generosity

LUK 6:38 See also 1CH 29:14; 2CO 9:6–9:11; PHP 4:18

Faithfulness

ROM 2:4 See also ISA 5:1–5:7; HOS 10:1–10:2, HOS 11:1–11:4

title: 1335: blessing
content:
Spiritual and material benefits given by God to be enjoyed. Blessing the name of God is also the appropriate response of believers to all that God has done for them.

God is the source of all blessing

1CH 29:11–29:12 See also PSA 89:11

At creation he blessed humans with fertility and authority over the earth

GEN 1:28–1:30

The disobedience of Adam and Eve caused God to remove his blessing

GEN 3:16–3:19 See also GEN 6:5–6:7

After the flood God renewed his promise of blessing to Noah

GEN 9:1–9:3 See also GEN 8:22; JOB 5:10; PSA 65:9–65:13

The blessings promised to Abraham

The blessings included descendants and nationhood

GEN 12:2–12:3 See also GEN 17:1–17:8, GEN 18:18, GEN 26:4, GEN 35:11; EXO 1:6–1:7; DEU 1:10–1:11, DEU 7:13; 1KI 3:8; NEH 9:23; ISA 51:2

The blessings included land

GEN 17:8 See also GEN 12:7, GEN 26:3; DEU 1:8, DEU 4:40, DEU 11:8–11:12

The promises of blessing to Abraham were fulfilled in Jesus Christ

GAL 3:16 See also ACT 3:25–3:26; GAL 3:29

Blessings promised to Israel

Blessings of fruitfulness and prosperity

DEU 7:13–7:14 See also GEN 49:25; LEV 26:10; DEU 15:4, DEU 28:8, DEU 28:12

Blessings of good health and long life

EXO 23:25–23:26 See also EXO 15:26; DEU 7:15

Blessings of peace and victory over enemies

DEU 28:7 See also EXO 23:22; LEV 26:6–26:8; DEU 7:24; PSA 29:11

The promise of being blessed by future restoration

JER 31:23 See also ISA 61:9, ISA 65:17–65:25; EZK 34:25–34:31

The conditions of God's blessings

Fear of the Lord brings blessing

JER 32:40 See also DEU 6:1–6:3, DEU 10:12–10:20; ECC 12:13; ISA 50:10

Obedience to the Lord brings blessing

DEU 11:26–11:28 See also DEU 28:1–28:14, DEU 30:15–30:20

To bless God is to worship and adore him

NEH 9:5 See also GEN 9:26, GEN 14:20

People may bless one another in the name of the Lord

NUM 6:24–6:26 There is a close link between blessing and prayer. See also GEN 27:27–27:29, GEN 49:1–49:28; RUT 1:8–1:9; 1SA 2:20; 1CH 4:10; MAT 23:39

title: 1340: consistency
content:
God's nature, plans and purpose do not change. The same consistent plan of salvation can be seen throughout the OT and NT, culminating in Jesus Christ who is “the same yesterday and today and for ever”. Believers are also called to show the same consistency in their faith and witness.

God's nature is consistent and unchanging

MAL 3:6 See also PSA 102:27; JAS 1:17

God's plans and purpose are consistent and unchanging

PSA 33:11 See also NUM 23:19; 1SA 15:29; JOB 36:5; PSA 89:34; TIT 1:2; HEB 7:21; PSA 110:4

God's plan of salvation is consistent

GEN 12:2–12:3; HEB 6:17–6:18, HEB 13:8 See also GEN 18:18, GEN 22:17–22:18, GEN 26:4, GEN 28:14; DEU 18:15, DEU 18:18; PSA 72:17; JER 4:1–4:2; ZEC 8:13; ACT 3:23–3:26; GAL 3:8

Believers are to live lives consistent with their profession of faith

EPH 4:1; PHP 1:27 See also MAT 5:37; 1TH 4:12; 1TI 3:7; HEB 13:7; JAS 5:12; 2PE 3:11

title: 1345: covenant
content: God's commitment to, and requirement of, his people expressed in promise, law, judgment, faithfulness and mercy. Also used of commitment within human relationships based upon agreements.

title: 1346: covenants, nature of
content:
A solemn agreement or promise, sometimes confirmed by sacrifice or by sharing in a meal, by which two or more parties commit themselves to the rights and responsibilities demanded by their relationship and their agreed course of action, and accept the serious consequences of breaking faith.

Kinds of covenant relationship

The relationship between king and people

2SA 5:3 parallel 1CH 11:3 See also 2SA 3:21; 2KI 11:17; 2CH 23:3

Terms for peace granted to a weaker party

JOS 9:15 See also 1SA 11:1; 1KI 15:19–15:20, 1KI 20:34; EZK 17:13–17:14

A mutual commitment to peaceful relations

1KI 5:12 See also GEN 21:27, GEN 26:28–26:29, GEN 31:44; AMO 1:9

An agreement on a common course of action

JER 34:8 See also 2KI 11:4 parallel 2CH 23:1; EZR 10:3; NEH 9:38; PSA 83:5

The relationship between husband and wife

PRO 2:17; ; MAL 2:14; JER 2:2; ; EZK 16:8

An expression of friendship

1SA 18:3 See also 1SA 20:16–20:17

Sealing a covenant

By sharing a meal

GEN 26:30, GEN 31:53–31:54; MAT 26:26–26:29 parallels MRK 14:22–14:25, LUK 22:17–22:20; ; 1CO 11:23–11:25

By offering a sacrifice

JER 34:18–34:19 The Hebrew expression for making a covenant means, literally, “to cut a covenant”. This may refer to the solemn practice, described here, of cutting an animal in half and walking between its pieces; ; GEN 15:9–15:18; ; EXO 24:4–24:8; ; PSA 50:5; ; HEB 12:24 Sacrifice is an important part in sealing covenants made by God.

By making an oath

GEN 21:31, GEN 26:31; ; JOS 9:15; ; 2KI 11:4; ; NEH 10:28–10:29

The obligations of a covenant

Covenant responsibilities must be honoured

NUM 30:2; 1SA 20:8 See also JOS 9:18; MAT 5:33–5:37; GAL 3:15

Covenant obligations are watched over by God

1SA 20:42 See also GEN 31:48–31:54; JOS 9:19; JER 34:15–34:16; MAL 2:14–2:16

The consequences of breaking covenant faith

JOS 9:20 See also JER 34:18–34:22; EZK 17:16–17:18; AMO 1:9

Covenants with other nations are forbidden

EXO 34:12 See also DEU 23:6; JOS 9:7

Covenants with other nations lead to a commitment to foreign gods

EXO 23:32–23:33, EXO 34:15–34:16; ; DEU 7:2–7:4

Covenants with other nations lead to a denial of faith in God

ISA 28:15, ISA 28:18, ISA 30:1–30:2, ISA 31:1; ; HOS 12:1

title: 1347: covenant, God's with Noah
content:
God's confirmation of, and commitment to maintain, his relationship with the natural order- implicit in the act of creation- whereby he promised never again to destroy the earth with a flood. This divine pledge, given unconditionally to Noah and to every living creature on earth, was accompanied by the sign of the rainbow.

The occasion of the covenant

The flood as divine judgment

GEN 6:17 See also GEN 6:5–6:7, GEN 6:11–6:13; 2PE 2:5

God's promise of salvation to Noah and his family

GEN 6:18 See also GEN 7:23, GEN 8:1, GEN 8:15–8:17; HEB 11:7; 1PE 3:20

God's promise never again to destroy the earth with a flood

GEN 9:11 See also GEN 8:21; ISA 54:9

The sign of the covenant

GEN 9:13 See also GEN 9:14–9:17

A universal covenant

God's relationship with every living creature

GEN 9:8–9:10 See also GEN 7:1–7:3; EZK 34:25; HOS 2:18; ZEC 11:10

God's relationship with the natural order

GEN 8:22; JER 33:25–33:26 God's relationship with the created order is here expressed as a covenant. It is probable that this is a reference to the Noahic covenant through which the implicit relationship between Creator and creation was confirmed. See also GEN 1:14, GEN 1:31, GEN 2:1; PSA 74:16–74:17; JER 5:24, JER 33:20–33:21

An everlasting covenant

GEN 9:16 See also GEN 9:12ISA 24:5 The reference here is to the Noahic covenant. Though this covenant was unconditional, the prohibition of bloodshed--often described as bringing pollution to the land--was associated with it. There may be, also, a wider reference to the effects of mankind's disregard for the norms laid down at creation. See also GEN 9:6; NUM 35:33; ISA 26:21

title: 1348: covenant, God's with Abraham
content:
God's gracious promise made to Abraham, and repeated to his descendants, to bless both them and, through them, the whole world. In response God calls for faithful obedience, expressed particularly in the outward sign of circumcision.

Features of the Abrahamic covenant

The covenant is based upon God's gracious promise

GAL 3:18 See also GEN 15:4–15:7, GEN 17:4–17:8, GEN 18:10, GEN 18:14; HEB 6:13–6:15

The covenant was confirmed by sacrifice

GEN 15:9–15:18

The covenant was given in perpetuity

GEN 17:7 See also GEN 17:13, GEN 17:19; 1CH 16:15–16:17 parallel PSA 105:8–105:10; JER 33:23–33:25

God's promises to Abraham

GEN 12:2–12:3

God promised the land of Canaan

GEN 17:8 See also GEN 12:7, GEN 15:18–15:21; EXO 6:4; JOS 1:3; NEH 9:8; ACT 7:5

God promised that Abraham would be the father of a nation

GEN 15:5 See also GEN 12:2, GEN 17:4–17:6 The change of name from Abram to Abraham is a sign of God's covenant with him; GEN 17:16, GEN 22:17; HEB 11:11–11:12

God promised a relationship with himself

GEN 17:8 See also GEN 26:24; DEU 29:13; MAT 22:32 parallel MRK 12:26; EXO 3:6; ACT 7:32

The requirements of the covenant

Obedience

GEN 17:9–17:14 Circumcision is the sign of Abraham's commitment to God's covenant with him; GEN 22:18 See also GEN 26:5; NUM 32:11 by contrast, the consequences of disobedience; HEB 11:8, HEB 11:17–11:19

Faith

GEN 15:6; NEH 9:8 See also ROM 4:3, ROM 4:11–4:12, ROM 4:18; GAL 3:6–3:7; HEB 11:8–11:12; JAS 2:23

God's faithfulness to the covenant

God remembers his promise

PSA 105:42 See also GEN 21:2, GEN 50:24; EXO 33:1; ACT 7:17; HEB 6:15

God shows compassion for his people

2KI 13:23 See also EXO 2:24–2:25, EXO 32:13–32:14; DEU 9:27; LUK 1:72–1:73

The scope of the covenant

The covenant continued through Isaac, not Ishmael

GEN 17:19–17:21 See also GEN 21:12; ROM 9:7–9:8; GAL 4:28

The covenant confirmed through Abraham, Isaac and Jacob

LEV 26:42 See also GEN 26:24 IsaacGEN 28:13–28:14, GEN 32:12 EXO 3:6; DEU 1:8; ACT 7:8

The people of Israel are heirs to the covenant

DEU 29:12–29:13 See also ACT 3:25

All nations will be blessed through Abraham

GEN 18:18 See also GEN 26:4; ACT 3:25; GAL 3:8–3:9, GAL 3:14, GAL 3:29

title: 1349: covenant, God's at Sinai
content:
God's faithful commitment, made in pursuance of his promises to Abraham, to acknowledge the newly-redeemed Israel as his own special people. Israel's required response to the grace of God in election was to be holiness and obedience to the law.

The occasion of the covenant

The covenant fulfilled God's promises to Abraham

DEU 29:12–29:13 See also EXO 2:24, EXO 6:4–6:8; DEU 7:8

The covenant followed Israel's redemption from slavery

JER 34:13 God's covenant at Sinai was made in the context of his historical acts of deliverance. Covenants (“treaties”) between a supreme (suzerain) overlord and a subordinate ruler (vassal) are well known in ancient Near Eastern writings. They are not “agreements” but “requirements”. See also EXO 20:2 parallel DEU 5:6; LEV 26:45

The covenant was mediated through Moses

EXO 34:27; ; LEV 26:46; ; DEU 29:1; ; JHN 1:17

The covenant was accompanied by signs of God's presence

EXO 19:18–19:19, EXO 20:18–20:19, EXO 24:16; ; PSA 68:8

Sealing the covenant

Sharing a meal

EXO 24:11

Offering a sacrifice

EXO 24:5–24:8; ; PSA 50:5

God's oath

DEU 28:9, DEU 29:14; ; EZK 16:8

Israel's promise

EXO 19:8, EXO 24:3, EXO 24:7

The covenant relationship

Israel as God's people

DEU 7:6 See also EXO 19:5–19:6; LEV 26:12; DEU 14:2; JER 13:11

Israel adopted into God's family

HOS 11:1 See also DEU 1:30–1:31, DEU 8:5; ISA 63:16; MAL 2:10

Israel as God's bride

JER 2:2 See also ISA 54:5–54:8; JER 31:32; EZK 16:8; HOS 2:14–2:16

The blessings of the covenant

Inheriting God's promises

DEU 6:3, DEU 26:18–26:19, DEU 28:9; ; JOS 23:5

Provision and protection

LEV 26:3–26:13; ; DEU 28:1–28:14 This pattern of blessings and curses is also a feature of Near Eastern political treaties.

The requirements of the covenant

Obedience to the law

EXO 24:7, EXO 34:27–34:28 See also DEU 4:13; JOS 8:31; NEH 8:1

Holiness

DEU 14:2 Israel has been made holy through her election as the covenant people of God. The ensuing call for holiness is a call to work out the implications of that relationship and to live in accordance with its demands; LEV 11:45 See also LEV 20:26

Wholehearted devotion

EXO 34:14; DEU 10:12 See also EXO 23:32; DEU 4:23, DEU 6:5, DEU 10:20; JOS 24:14–24:15

Breaking the covenant

The consequences of breaking the covenant

title: 1350: covenant, God's with Israel's priests
content:
God's promise to the Levites, and to the family of Phinehas in particular, to maintain those who serve him faithfully in a lasting priesthood.

God's covenant with Phinehas

NUM 25:12–25:13 Phinehas was the grandson of Aaron. God's pledge of “a lasting priesthood”, granted to him as a result of his zeal, probably means that the high priesthood would remain in his family. See also NUM 25:10–25:11; EZR 7:1–7:5; PSA 106:30–106:31

God's covenant with the Levites

An everlasting covenant

NUM 18:19 This covenant confirms the privileges and responsibilities of Aaron and his fellow Levites who have been called to Israel's priesthood. The reference to “salt” may underline the permanent nature of the covenant. God's covenant with David is also described as a “covenant of salt”. See also 2CH 13:5; JER 33:18–33:22

Requirements of the covenant

MAL 2:5–2:6

The Levites' portion is the LORD

DEU 10:8–10:9 See also NUM 3:12

The covenant violated

title: 1351: covenant, God's with David
content:
God's promise to establish David and his descendants on Israel's throne for ever. It provided Israel with a basis for the hope of deliverance and restoration, and became a focus for the Messianic expectation which was fulfilled, ultimately, in Jesus Christ.

God's promise to establish David's line

God's election of David

PSA 78:70 See also 2SA 6:21; 1KI 8:16 parallel 2CH 6:6

God's covenant is everlasting

PSA 89:3–89:4 See also 2SA 7:11–7:16, 2SA 23:5; 1KI 2:45; 2CH 13:5; PSA 18:50, PSA 89:28–89:29, PSA 89:35–89:37; JER 33:17

God's covenant is inherited through obedience

PSA 132:11–132:12 Though God's promise to maintain David's throne is unbreakable, individual descendants must continue to be faithful if they are to inherit the blessings promised to David. See also 1KI 8:25–8:26 parallel 2CH 6:16–6:17; 1KI 9:4–9:5 parallel 2CH 7:17–7:18

God's covenant blessings are forfeited through disobedience

JER 22:4–22:5 Failure to remain faithful to God brings judgment on the royal house and the people. See also 1KI 9:6–9:9 parallel 2CH 7:19–7:22; 1KI 11:11–11:13, 1KI 11:31–11:33; JER 7:24–7:26, JER 22:6–22:9, JER 36:30–36:31

God's promise is fulfilled by grace

2CH 21:7 parallel 2KI 8:19 Though particular kings may suffer God's anger because of their sin, God's faithfulness to the house of David as a whole, and thus to the nation of Israel, will continue. See also 1KI 11:34–11:36, 1KI 11:39, 1KI 15:4; PSA 89:30–89:34

The Davidic covenant as a basis for hope

God's election of Jerusalem

2KI 21:7 parallel 2CH 33:7 God's Name signifies his presence. God's choice of David was closely related to his choice of Jerusalem, the city established by David, as the place where the divine presence symbolised in the temple and the ark of the covenant was to be found. See also 1KI 8:20–8:21 parallel 2CH 6:10–6:11; 1KI 11:32, 1KI 11:36; 1CH 23:25; 2CH 6:41–6:42 parallel PSA 132:8–132:10

title: 1352: covenant, the new
content:
The fulfilment of God's purposes of salvation expressed in the covenants of the OT, mediated by Jesus Christ and sealed in his blood. It is a covenant of grace, the benefits of which include forgiveness, a renewed relationship with God and, through the Holy Spirit, an inward transformation that enables obedience to its demands and so ensures that it will not again be broken. JER 31:31 The “new” covenant is contrasted with the covenant made at Sinai which is sometimes referred to as the “old” or “first” covenant.

The new covenant fulfils the OT covenants

God's covenant with Noah

ISA 54:9–54:10; ; HOS 2:18

God's covenant with Abraham

God's covenant at Sinai

EZK 16:60, EZK 16:62, EZK 20:37

2CO 3:9; ; GAL 3:13–3:14

Under the new covenant the requirements of obedience are not abolished, but in writing his laws on human hearts, God gives the ability to meet the covenant demands. See also JER 32:38–32:40; EZK 11:19–11:20; 2CO 3:3; HEB 8:9–8:10, HEB 9:14, HEB 10:16

A renewed relationship with God

EZK 37:26–37:27 “peace” refers to spiritual and material well-being; real peace is possible only for those in a right relationship with God. The formula “I will be their God, and they will be my people” is associated also with the Abrahamic and especially the Sinaitic covenants. See also JER 24:7, JER 31:1; EZK 34:30–34:31; HOS 2:19–2:23

title: 1403: God, revelation of
content:

title: 1415: miracles
content: Events that are totally out of the ordinary and that cannot be adequately explained on the basis of natural occurrences, such as those associated with the ministry of Jesus Christ. They are seen as evidence of the presence and power of God in the world or as demonstrating authority on the part of one of his servants.

title: 1416: miracles, nature of
content:

title: 1418: miracles, responses to
content:

1KI 17:13–17:15 The widow gave Elijah her last meal before she could be miraculously provided for. content:
Speaking authoritatively of the future. Since God knows the future he is able to reveal it to people beforehand. Predictions, which are often but not necessarily contained in prophecies, should not be confused with promises or expressions of intention.

title: 1427: prophecy
content: The disclosing of the will and purposes of God through inspired or Spirit-filled human beings. The OT emphasises the importance of prophecy as a means of knowing God. Many OT prophecies find their fulfilment in Jesus Christ. The NT sets out the place of prophecy in the life of the church and gives guidance concerning the use of this gift.

title: 1428: prophecy, inspiration of OT
content:
The prophecies of biblical messengers are acknowledged as being of divine origin, inspired through the activity of the Holy Spirit.

God speaks through his prophets

2PE 1:20–1:21 See also NEH 9:30; ZEC 7:12; HEB 1:1

God is the source of prophecy

EZK 24:20–24:21 The phrase “the word of the LORD came to ...” is frequently used of (and by) the prophets to indicate that the message they proclaimed came directly from God. See also 1SA 15:10; 2SA 7:4; 1KI 13:20, 1KI 16:1, 1KI 17:2; 2KI 20:4; JER 1:2; EZK 6:1; JON 1:1; HAG 2:10; ZEC 1:1

Prophets inspired by God speak the truth

DEU 18:22 See also JER 28:9

content:
Prophets relayed their messages from God in a variety of ways, choosing methods that would make the meaning clear, attract attention or convince the people of the truth of their words.

See also 2KI 20:16–20:18; JER 7:2, JER 30:3, JER 44:26; EZK 34:7–34:10; HOS 4:1; AMO 1:5, AMO 7:16–7:17; ZEP 3:20; HAG 2:9; ZEC 1:3; MAL 1:6

content:
The NT describes the prophetic aspect of the ministry of Jesus Christ, and also recognises that prophetic gifts are given to believers through the Holy Spirit. It provides guidance on how such prophetic gifts are to be used.

Prophecy superior to speaking in tongues

1CO 14:5

Prophecy in the early church

title: 1436: reality
content:
Scripture provides an accurate and reliable guide to the realities of the spiritual and material worlds. It affirms the reality of human sin, of the power and purpose of God to deal with sin through Jesus Christ and of the guidance of the Holy Spirit.

The reality of God

The reality of the existence of God

title: 1439: revelation
content: The making known of God's person, nature and deeds, in Scripture, history and supremely the person of Jesus Christ. God is also made known, to a limited yet important extent, through his creation.

title: 1440: revelation, through creation
content:
The creation bears witness to the wisdom and power of its creator. This natural knowledge of God is limited in its extent, but is sufficient to convince human beings of the existence of God and the need to respond to him.

The creation bears witness to its creator

PSA 19:1–19:6 See also JOB 36:22–37:18; AMO 4:13; ACT 14:15–14:17

All human beings have a natural awareness of God

ROM 2:14–2:15 See also ACT 17:22–17:31

The limitations of a natural knowledge of God

ROM 1:18–1:21 See also ROM 1:32; 1CO 1:20–1:21

title: 1441: revelation, necessity of
content:
Finiteness and sin make it impossible to gain adequate knowledge of God through human effort alone. God in mercy makes himself known through the incarnation of the Son and the illumination of human minds to understand him.

The impossibility of fully knowing God without revelation

God is beyond unaided human knowing

JHN 1:18 See also EXO 33:20; ISA 55:8–55:9; JHN 6:46; 1JN 4:12

The human mind is limited

JOB 11:7 See also JOB 9:4, JOB 9:10, JOB 23:3–23:9, JOB 26:14, JOB 36:26, JOB 37:5, JOB 37:23; PSA 139:6, PSA 145:3; ECC 3:11; ISA 40:13–40:14, ISA 40:28; ROM 11:33

The human mind cannot discern God of its own accord

2CO 4:4 See also JHN 1:5; ROM 1:18–1:32; 1CO 1:21, 1CO 2:14; 2CO 3:14; EPH 4:17–4:18

God is known fully only through Jesus Christ

HEB 1:1–1:2; 1JN 5:20 See also JHN 1:14–1:18, JHN 17:3; COL 1:15–1:20

title: 1443: revelation, in OT
content:
The OT bears witness to God's revelation in the history of Israel and in the inspired testimony of the prophets and other writers of the period. This knowledge of God prepares the way for the full disclosure of God in Jesus Christ in the NT.

GEN 1:3, GEN 2:16–2:18 See also GEN 1:29–1:30, GEN 3:9–3:19, GEN 4:6–4:7, GEN 4:9–4:15, GEN 6:13–6:22

God's covenant with Abraham

Scripture

content:

The unity and progress of revelation

The unity of OT and NT

2CO 4:4; COL 1:15

Jesus Christ has the nature of God

God commands that his word be heeded

title: 1448: signs
content: Miraculous or supernatural confirmations of the existence and power of God or the miraculous demonstration of spiritual authority on the part of an individual.

title: 1449: signs, purposes of
content:

title: 1460: truth
content: Truth in Scripture is more than mere veracity. In the OT it is a moral concept, grounded in the being of God himself, which embodies the ideas of faithfulness and reliability, while in the NT the concept widens to embrace the ideas of reality and completeness.

title: 1461: truth, nature of
content:
God is the essence of truth and this quality should be reflected in the character of his children. Falsehood is condemned in Scripture, particularly in legal contexts, but truth is praised and advocated. God takes pleasure in truth and honesty, which are marks of an upright person.

Truth originates with God

PSA 43:3 See also PSA 25:5, PSA 26:3, PSA 86:11; ISA 65:16; DAN 9:13

Truth is an aspect of God's character

It is demonstrated in his faithfulness and reliability

title: 1500: God, the Trinity
content: • 1510: Trinity, the

title: 1510: Trinity, the
content: The characteristically Christian doctrine about God. It declares that there is only one true God; that this God is three persons, the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit, each of whom is distinct from, yet interrelated with, the others; and that all three persons are fully, equally and eternally divine.

title: 1511: Trinity, relationships between the persons
content:
The actual term “the Trinity” is not found in Scripture, but the truths implied in a trinitarian understanding of God are clearly set out. The OT hints at a plurality of persons in the Godhead. The NT affirms that the Son and the Holy Spirit are divine.

There is only one God

DEU 6:4 See also ISA 43:10–43:11, ISA 44:8; 1TI 1:17, 1TI 2:5; JAS 2:19

OT indications of plurality in the Godhead

God refers to himself in the plural

GEN 1:26 See also GEN 3:22, GEN 11:7; ISA 6:8

The angel of the LORD

GEN 16:11–16:13 The “angel of the LORD” is identified with, yet distinct from, God. See also GEN 18:1–18:33; EXO 3:2–3:6; JDG 13:3–13:22

The word of God

The word of the LORD'' orwisdom of God'' is personified and identified with, yet distinct from, God: PSA 33:4; ; PRO 8:22–8:31

The Spirit of God

The Spirit of God is God's personal agent: GEN 1:2; ; NEH 9:20; ; JOB 33:4; ; ISA 40:13

The Messiah

The Messiah's divine nature is emphasised: PSA 110:1; ; ISA 9:6; ; JER 23:5–23:6

Interchangeable expressions

Word, Spirit (or breath) and LORD are used interchangeably for God: PSA 33:6; ; ISA 48:16, ISA 61:1

NT trinitarian references

MAT 28:19 The unity of the three persons is reflected in the singular name. See also 2CO 13:14; EPH 4:4–4:6; REV 1:4–1:5

The unity of the three persons

The Son is fully united with the Father

title: 1512: Trinity, equality of the persons
content:
The Son and the Holy Spirit are equal to God the Father in eternity, nature and status. Within the Trinity the Father is head, first among equals; the Son and the Holy Spirit do the Father's will, glorifying him and making him known; and the Holy Spirit glorifies and makes known the Son.

The Son is equal with the Father

JHN 5:18 See also JHN 5:23, JHN 8:16, JHN 14:9, JHN 17:10; PHP 2:6; COL 2:9ROM 1:7 The equality of the Son and the Father is often implied in the opening greetings of the NT letters. See also 1CO 1:3; GAL 1:3; EPH 1:2; 1TH 1:1; 1TI 1:2; PHM 1:3; 2PE 1:2REV 5:13 Doxologies are addressed to the Father and the Son equally or just to the Son. See also ROM 9:5; HEB 13:21; 2PE 3:18; REV 7:10

content:

title: 1610: Scripture
content: The biblical writings, inspired by the Holy Spirit, have been entrusted to the church to remind it of the central teachings of the gospel, to guard it from error and to enable it to grow into holiness. The church is required to be obedient to Scripture and revere it as the Word of God.

title: 1611: Scripture, inspiration and authority of
content:
Those writings that are acknowledged to be the word of God to be revered as issuing from him and as having his authority.

Recognition of a body of sacred writings

In the OT

NEH 8:1 See also EXO 24:7; JOS 8:34; 2KI 22:8 parallel 2CH 34:14; 2KI 23:2 parallel 2CH 34:30; 2CH 35:12; EZR 6:18; NEH 8:8, NEH 9:3, NEH 13:1

By Jesus Christ

MAT 22:29 See also LUK 4:21, LUK 24:27, LUK 24:45MAT 21:13 parallel LUK 19:46 Jesus Christ frequently appealed to the OT as a recognised sacred corpus, with the expression “it is written”. See also ISA 56:7; JER 7:11; MAT 4:4 parallel LUK 4:4; DEU 8:3; MAT 4:7 parallel LUK 4:12; DEU 6:16; MAT 4:10 parallel LUK 4:8; DEU 6:13; MAT 21:42 parallel MRK 12:10; PSA 118:22–118:23; MAT 26:31 parallel MRK 14:27; ZEC 13:7; MRK 7:6–7:7; ISA 29:13; LUK 7:27; MAL 3:1; JHN 7:38

title: 1613: Scripture, purpose of
content:
Scripture has been given by God to lead people to faith and salvation. Through Scripture believers are nurtured in faith and led to spiritual maturity.

Scripture is intended to lead people to salvation

2TI 3:14–3:15 See also PSA 19:7–19:11; JHN 20:30–20:31; ROM 10:8

Scripture is intended to lead believers to maturity in faith

By its teaching

2TI 3:16 See also DEU 6:6–6:9; PSA 19:7–19:8, PSA 119:9, PSA 119:130; COL 3:16

By its rebuke and correction

2TI 3:16 See also PSA 19:11–19:13; 1CO 10:11–10:12; HEB 4:12–4:13

By training in righteousness

title: 1630: Book of the Covenant
content:
The customary designation given to the code of law in Ex 20:22–23:33, but it may also have included the Ten Commandments (Ex 20:2–17).

Moses reads the Book of the Covenant to the Israelites

EXO 24:7 See also EXO 20:22–23:33 The Book of the Covenant is the oldest, extant codification of Israelite law, where human values are constantly elevated over material ones. It can be argued that each section of the code is a further exposition of one of the Ten Commandments, giving specific case laws.

Josiah reads the rediscovered Book of the Covenant to the people of Judah

2KI 23:2–23:3 parallel 2CH 34:30–34:31 See also 2KI 23:21 This reference to the Book of the Covenant applies either to all or part of the book of Deuteronomy, or to the entire Mosaic Law.

title: 1640: Book of the Law
content:
A documentary form of the Law placed beside the ark of the covenant as a reminder to Israel of her covenant obligation. It was rediscovered and applied during the reign of King Josiah of Judah and read publicly by Ezra after the rebuilding of the walls of Jerusalem.

The Book of the Law was written by Moses

DEU 31:24–31:26 The book in question could be the material now included as chapters 1–30, although some scholars suggest it might refer to the material now included as chapters 12–26. See also DEU 28:58–28:61, DEU 29:18–29:21, DEU 30:10

The Book of the Law was passed from Moses to Joshua

JOS 1:7–1:8 See also JOS 8:30–8:31, JOS 8:34–8:35, JOS 23:6, JOS 24:26

The Book of the Law during the monarchy

2KI 14:6 parallel 2CH 25:4; ; 2CH 17:9

The Book of the Law was rediscovered and applied in Josiah's reign

2KI 22:8–22:16 parallel 2CH 34:15–34:24 A reference to part or all of Deuteronomy or possibly the entire Pentateuch (Genesis to Deuteronomy).

The Book of the Law was read publicly by Ezra

NEH 8:1–8:18 See also NEH 9:3

Paul refers to the Book of the Law

GAL 3:10 Paul is teaching that justification is by faith. See also DEU 27:26

title: 1650: numbers, significance of
content: Numbers appear frequently in Scripture and in a variety of contexts. Occasionally they have a special theological significance.

title: 1651: numbers, 1–2
content:

title: 1652: numbers, 3–5
content:
The number three, primarily associated with the doctrine of the Trinity, is also associated with periods of specially significant divine activity (e.g., “on the third day”). Four and five have a variety of associations.

Three

The Trinity

MAT 28:19 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26; 1CO 12:4–12:6; 1PE 1:2

The triad of faith, hope and love

1CO 13:13 See also 1TH 1:3

Three days

GEN 30:36 Three days is a common period, often indicating the length of a journey or a period leading to a critical point. See also GEN 40:12–40:22; EXO 3:18, EXO 10:22; NUM 10:33; JDG 14:14; 1KI 12:5 parallel 2CH 10:5

title: 1655: numbers, hundreds and thousands

title: 1660: Sermon on the Mount
content:
A collection of Jesus Christ's teaching on the theme of discipleship. It deals with the righteousness required of disciples, the way they are to perform their religious duties, and the blessing and persecution they will know as followers of Christ. It can be seen as a manifesto setting out the nature of life in the kingdom of God.

The setting of the Sermon on the Mount

MAT 5:1; LUK 6:17 Either Jesus Christ taught similar material in two different locations, as is common in preaching, or else Luke more specifically identifies that he taught on a level place or plateau on the mountainside.

The teaching of the Sermon on the Mount

The blessings of being in the kingdom

See also MAT 5:3–5:12 parallel LUK 6:20–6:23

Jesus Christ's disciples are to have a positive influence on the world

MAT 5:13–5:16

The relationship between Jesus Christ's message and the law

MAT 5:17–5:21

Jesus Christ's disciples are to exhibit a new depth of righteousness

MAT 5:20 See also MAT 5:48

The righteousness required of Jesus Christ disciples

Not only murder but also anger and broken relationships are wrong: MAT 5:21–5:24; ; EXO 20:13; ; MAT 5:25–5:26 parallel LUK 12:58–12:59; MAT 5:27–5:30; ; EXO 20:14; MAT 5:31–5:32 faithfulness in marriage is called for; ; MAT 5:33–5:37 faithfulness in speech is called for; ; MAT 5:38–5:48 Retaliation must be abandoned, and enemies as well as neighbours must be loved.

Jesus Christ's disciples are to seek only God's approval in their religious duties

MAT 6:1

How religious duties are to be performed

See also MAT 6:2–6:4 Giving to the poor is to be done secretly; MAT 6:5–6:8 Prayer is not to become a public display; MAT 6:16–6:18 Fasting is to be done without show.

The kingdom is to be valued above material possessions

MAT 6:19–6:21 See also MAT 6:24–6:34 parallel LUK 12:22–12:31

Jesus Christ's disciples are to avoid being judgmental

MAT 7:1–7:2 See also MAT 7:3–7:5 parallel LUK 6:41–6:42

Jesus Christ's disciples should pray, confident of an answer

MAT 7:7–7:8 parallel LUK 11:9–11:10 See also MAT 7:9–7:12 parallel LUK 11:11–11:13

A pattern for prayer

MAT 6:9–6:15 parallel LUK 11:2–11:4

True disciples will obey Jesus Christ teaching

MAT 7:21 See also MAT 7:15–7:20, MAT 7:24–7:27 parallel LUK 6:47–6:49

Characteristics of Jesus Christ's teaching in the Sermon on the Mount

MAT 7:28–7:29

title: 1680: types
content:
An OT institution, person, place or event regarded as anticipating the person of Jesus Christ or some aspect of the Christian faith or life.

OT foreshadowings of Jesus Christ

OT institutions as types

The Sabbath: Jesus Christ's finished work

HEB 4:3–4:6 See also GEN 2:2–2:3; HEB 4:9–4:10

Marriage: Jesus Christ's union with his people

EPH 5:30–5:32 See also GEN 2:22–2:24 Adam may be viewed as a figure of Jesus Christ, and Eve, of the church, formed in Christ, loved and united with him; PSA 45:10–45:11; MAT 9:15, MAT 25:1; JHN 3:29; EPH 5:25–5:27; REV 19:7, REV 21:2

JHN 1:14 The context shows that “The Word” is Jesus Christ; “made his dwelling” involves the idea of pitching a tent and reflects the tabernacle which was pitched among the Israelites and filled with God's glory; so Christ has pitched his tent among his people and revealed his glory. See also EXO 25:8, EXO 40:34–40:35
